1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: O YOU PEOPLE, fear your Lord. The great upheaval of the Hour will indeed be terrible.

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: O people, fear your Lord; indeed the earthquake of the Last Day is a tremendous thing.

1.author: Arberry
1.text: O men, fear your Lord! Surely the earthquake of the Hour is a mighty thing;

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Mankind! fear your Lord; verily the quake of the Hour shall be a thing mighty.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: O mankind! Fear your Lord and be dutiful to Him! Verily, the earthquake of the Hour (of Judgement) is a terrible thing.

1.author: Itani
1.text: O people, be conscious of your Lord. The quaking of the Hour is a tremendous thing.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: O mankind, fear the (wrath of) your Lord! Indeed, the earthquake of the Hour (of Judgement) will be an awesome thing.

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: O mankind! Have Taqwa of your Lord! Verily, the earthquake of the Hour is a terrible thing.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: O mankind! Fear your Lord. Lo! the earthquake of the Hour (of Doom) is a tremendous thing.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: O mankind! Be wary of your Lord! Indeed the quake of the Hour is a terrible thing.

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: O people, have fear of your Lord. The earthquake of the Hour shall be a great thing.

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: O mankind, fear your Lord. Indeed, the convulsion of the [final] Hour is a terrible thing.

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: People, have fear of your Lord; the quake (of the physical realm) at the Hour of Doom will be terribly violent.

1.author: Shakir
1.text: O people! guard against (the punishment from) your Lord; surely the violence of the hour is a grievous thing.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: O People! Fear your Lord. The catastrophe of the Last Hour shall be terrible indeed:

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: O mankind! fear your Lord! for the convulsion of the Hour (of Judgment) will be a thing terrible!

1.author: Transliteration
1.text: Ya ayyuha alnnasu ittaqoo rabbakum inna zalzalata alssaAAati shayon AAatheemun

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: The day you see it every suckling female will forget her suckling, and every pregnant female will discharge her burden. You will see men drunk, yet it will not be intoxication. The torment of God will be severe.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: On the day when you will witness it, every nursing mother will forget her nurseling and every pregnant one will discharge her burden, and you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) will see people as if they are drunk, whereas they will not be intoxicated, but the fact is that Allah’s punishment is very severe.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: on the day when you behold it, every suckling woman shall neglect the child she has suckled, and every pregnant woman shall deposit her burden, and thou shalt see mankind drunk, yet they are not drunk, but God's chastisement is terrible.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: The Day whereon ye behold it, every suckling woman shall forget that which she suckleth, and every burthened woman shall lay down her burthen; and thou shalt behold mankind as drunken, whereas drunken they will be not, but the torment of Allah shall be severe.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: The Day you shall see it, every nursing mother will forget her nursling, and every pregnant one will drop her load, and you shall see mankind as in a drunken state, yet they will not be drunken, but severe will be the Torment of Allah.

2.author: Itani
2.text: On the Day when you will see it: every nursing mother will discard her infant, and every pregnant woman will abort her load, and you will see the people drunk, even though they are not drunk—but the punishment of God is severe.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: On the Day when you witness it, the suckling woman shall utterly neglect the infant she suckles, and every pregnant woman shall cast her burden, and you will see people as though they are drunk, when they are not drunk; but dreadful shall be Allah's chastisement.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: The Day you shall see it, every nursing mother will forget her nursling, and every pregnant one will drop her load, and you shall see mankind as in a drunken state, yet they will not be drunk, but Allah's torment is severe.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: On the day when ye behold it, every nursing mother will forget her nursling and every pregnant one will be delivered of her burden, and thou (Muhammad) wilt see mankind as drunken, yet they will not be drunken, but the Doom of Allah will be strong (upon them).

2.author: Qarai
2.text: The day that you will see it, every suckling female will be unmindful what she suckled, and every pregnant female will deliver her burden, and you will see the people drunk, yet they will not be drunken, but Allah’s punishment is severe.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: On that Day you will see every one that suckles shall forsake her suckling, and every carrier shall miscarry, and you shall see mankind drunk although they arenot drunk; dreadful will be the punishment of Allah.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: On the Day you see it every nursing mother will be distracted from that [child] she was nursing, and every pregnant woman will abort her pregnancy, and you will see the people [appearing] intoxicated while they are not intoxicated; but the punishment of Allah is severe.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: When that hour comes, every breast-feeding mother will drop her baby out of fear and every pregnant female will cast off her burden. You will see the people behaving as though they were drunk, while, in fact, they are not drunk. They only will look such because of the severity of God's torment.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: On the day when you shall see it, every woman giving suck shall quit in confusion what she suckled, and every pregnant woman shall lay down her burden, and you shall see men intoxicated, and they shall not be intoxicated but the chastisement of Allah will be severe.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: when that Day comes, every suckling mother shall forsake her infant and every pregnant woman shall cast her burden and everyone will appear intoxicated, although they are not: the punishment of God will be severe indeed.

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: The Day ye shall see it, every mother giving suck shall forget her suckling-babe, and every pregnant female shall drop her load (unformed): thou shalt see mankind as in a drunken riot, yet not drunk: but dreadful will be the Wrath of Allah.

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Yawma tarawnaha tathhalu kullu murdiAAatin AAamma ardaAAat watadaAAu kullu thati hamlin hamlaha watara alnnasa sukara wama hum bisukara walakinna AAathaba Allahi shadeedun

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: And yet there are men who contend about God without understanding, and follow every wayward devil

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And among people are some who argue concerning Allah without knowing, and blindly follow every rebellious devil.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: And among men there is such a one that disputes concerning God without knowledge and follows every rebel Satan,

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And of mankind is he who disputeth respecting Allah without knowledge, and followeth any Satan froward.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And among mankind is he who disputes concerning Allah, without knowledge, and follows every rebellious (disobedient to Allah) Shaitan (devil) (devoid of each and every kind of good).

3.author: Itani
3.text: Among the people is he who argues about God without knowledge, and follows every defiant devil.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: Among people there are some who wrangle about Allah without knowledge and follow every rebellious devil,

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: And among mankind is he who disputes about Allah, without knowledge, and follows every rebellious Shaytan.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Among mankind is he who disputeth concerning Allah without knowledge, and followeth each froward devil;

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Among the people are those who dispute about Allah without any knowledge, and follow every froward devil,

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: Among people there are those, who without knowledge, dispute about Allah and follow every rebel satan,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And of the people is he who disputes about Allah without knowledge and follows every rebellious devil.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: Some people argue about God without knowledge and follow every rebellious devil.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And among men there is he who disputes about Allah without knowledge and follows every rebellious Shaitan;

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: Yet there are some who dispute about God without having any knowledge and they follow every rebellious devil,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: And yet among men there are such as dispute about Allah, without knowledge, and follow every evil one obstinate in rebellion!

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Wamina alnnasi man yujadilu fee Allahi bighayri AAilmin wayattabiAAu kulla shaytanin mareedin

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Who, it is inscribed, will beguile whoever follows him, and lead him to the torment of Hell.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: (The devil) Upon whom is decreed that whoever befriends him, he will certainly mislead him and show him the path to hell.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: against whom it is written down that whosoever takes him for a friend, him he leads astray, and he guides him to the chastisement of the burning.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Against whom it is prescribed, that: whosoever befriendeth him, him he shall lead astray and shall guide him on to the torment of the Flame.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: For him (the devil) it is decreed that whosoever follows him, he will mislead him, and will drive him to the torment of the Fire. [Tafsir At-Tabari]

4.author: Itani
4.text: It was decreed for him, that whoever follows him—he will misguide him, and lead him to the torment of the Blaze.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: although it is decreed about him that he shall lead into error whosoever takes him for a friend, and will direct him to the torment of the Fire.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: For him (the devil) it is decreed that whosoever follows him, he will mislead him, and will drive him to the torment of the Fire.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: For him it is decreed that whoso taketh him for friend, he verily will mislead him and will guide him to the punishment of the Flame.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: about whom it has been decreed that he will mislead those who take him for an ally, and conduct them toward the punishment of the Blaze.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: against whom it is written down that whosoever takes him for a guide, he guides astray, and leads him to the punishment of the Blaze.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: It has been decreed for every devil that whoever turns to him - he will misguide him and will lead him to the punishment of the Blaze.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: It has been decided that satan will mislead and submit anyone who establishes friendship with him to the torment of the burning fire.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Against him it is written down that whoever takes him for a friend, he shall lead him astray and conduct him to the chastisement of the burning fire.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: it has been decreed concerning anyone whom he befriends, that he shall mislead him and guide him to the punishment of the Fire.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: About the (Evil One) it is decreed that whoever turns to him for friendship, him will he lead astray, and he will guide him to the Penalty of the Fire.

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Kutiba AAalayhi annahu man tawallahu faannahu yudilluhu wayahdeehi ila AAathabi alssaAAeeri

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: If you have any doubt, O men, about being raised to life again, (remember) that We created you from dust, then a drop of semen, then an embryo, then a chewed up lump of flesh shaped and shapeless, that We may reveal (the various steps) to you. We keep what We please in the womb for a certain time, then you come out as a child, then reach the prime of age. Some of you die, some reach the age of dotage when they forget what they knew, having known it once. You see the earth all withered, then We send down rain upon it, and it bestirs itself, swells, and brings forth every kind of beauteous verdure.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: O people, if you doubt your revival on the Day of Resurrection, then ponder that We created you from dust, then from a drop of liquid, then from a clot, then from a piece of flesh formed and without form, so that We show you Our signs for you; and We keep whomever We want inside the mothers’ wombs up to an appointed time, then extract you as infants, then in order that you reach your puberty; and among you is one who dies earlier, and among you is one put to the most abject age, so after having knowledge, knows nothing; and you see the earth desolate, then when We sent down water upon it, it freshened up and developed and produced beautiful pairs of all kinds.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: O men, if you are in doubt as to the Uprising, surely We created you of dust then of a sperm-drop, then of a blood clot, then of a lump of flesh, formed and unformed that We may make clear to you. And We establish in the wombs what We will, till a stated term, then We deliver you as infants, then that you may come of age; and some of you die, and some of you are kept back unto the vilest state of life, that after knowing somewhat, they may know nothing. And thou beholdest the earth blackened, then, when We send down water upon it, it quivers, and swells, and puts forth herbs of every joyous kind.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: O Mankind! if ye be in doubt respecting the Resurrection, then We have created you of the dust, then of a drop, then of clot, then of a piece of flesh, formed and unformed, that We might manifest unto you Our power. And We settle in the wombs that which We will until a term determined. Then We bring you forth as babes, then We let you reach your maturity. And of you is he who dieth, and of you is he who is brought back to the most abject age, so that after knowing he knoweth not aught. Andthou beholdest the earth withered up, and when We send down thereon water, it stirreth and swelleth, and it groweth every luxuriant kind of growth.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: O mankind! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection, then verily! We have created you (i.e. Adam) from dust, then from a Nutfah (mixed drops of male and female sexual discharge i.e. offspring of Adam), then from a clot (a piece of thick coagulated blood) then from a little lump of flesh, some formed and some unformed (miscarriage), that We may make (it) clear to you (i.e. to show you Our Power and Ability to do what We will). And We cause whom We will to remain in the wombs for an appointed term, then We bring you out as infants, then (give you growth) that you may reach your age of full strength. And among you there is he who dies (young), and among you there is he who is brought back to the miserable old age, so that he knows nothing after having known. And you see the earth barren, but when We send down water (rain) on it, it is stirred (to life), it swells and puts forth every lovely kind (of growth).

5.author: Itani
5.text: O people! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection—We created you from dust, then from a small drop, then from a clinging clot, then from a lump of flesh, partly developed and partly undeveloped. In order to clarify things for you. And We settle in the wombs whatever We will for a designated term, and then We bring you out as infants, until you reach your full strength. And some of you will pass away, and some of you will be returned to the vilest age, so that he may not know, after having known. And you see the earth still; but when We send down water on it, it vibrates, and swells, and grows all kinds of lovely pairs.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: O mankind! If you have any doubt concerning Resurrection, then know that it is surely We Who created you from dust, then from a drop of sperm, then from a clot of blood, then from a little lump of flesh, some of it shapely and other shapeless. (We are rehearsing this) that We may make the reality clear to you. We cause (the drop of sperm) that We please to remain in the wombs till an appointed time. We bring you forth as infants (and nurture you) that you may come of age. Among you is he that dies (at a young age) and he who is kept back to the most abject age so that after once having known, he reaches a stage when he knows nothing. You see the earth dry and barren and then no sooner than We send down water upon it, it begins to quiver and swell and brings forth every kind of beauteous vegetation.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: O mankind! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection, then verily, We have created you from dust, then from a Nutfah, then from a clot then from a little lump of flesh -- some formed and some unformed -- that We may make (it) clear to you. And We cause whom We will to remain in the wombs for an appointed term, then We bring you out as infants, then (give you growth) that you may reach your age of full strength. And among you there is he who dies (young), and among you there is he who is brought back to the miserable old age, so that he knows nothing after having known. And you see the earth Hamidatan, but when We send down water on it, it is stirred (to life), and it swells and puts forth every lovely kind (of growth).

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: O mankind! if ye are in doubt concerning the Resurrection, then lo! We have created you from dust, then from a drop of seed, then from a clot, then from a little lump of flesh shapely and shapeless, that We may make (it) clear for you. And We cause what We will to remain in the wombs for an appointed time, and afterward We bring you forth as infants, then (give you growth) that ye attain your full strength. And among you there is he who dieth (young), and among you there is he who is brought back to the most abject time of life, so that, after knowledge, he knoweth naught. And thou (Muhammad) seest the earth barren, but when We send down water thereon, it doth thrill and swell and put forth every lovely kind (of growth).

5.author: Qarai
5.text: O people! If you are in doubt about the resurrection, [consider that] We created you from dust, then from a drop of [seminal] fluid, then from a clinging mass, then from a fleshy tissue, partly formed and partly unformed, so that We may manifest [Our power] to you. We lodge in the wombs whatever [fetus] We wish for a specified term, then We bring you forth as infants, then [We rear you] so that you may come of age. [Then] there are some of you who are taken away, and there are some of you who are relegated to the nethermost age, such that he knows nothing after [having possessed] some knowledge. And you see the earth torpid, yet when We send down water upon it, it stirs and swells, and grows every delightful kind [of plant].

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: O people, if you are in doubt about the Resurrection, remember that We first created you from dust, then, from a sperm drop, then from a clot, and then from a bitesize tissue formed and unformed, so that We might clarify for you. We establish in the wombs whatever We will for an appointed term, and then We bring you forth as infants, then you come of age. Some of you die, and some of you are kept back to the vilest state of life, after knowing somewhat, they know nothing. And you see the earth dry; but no sooner do We send down rain upon it than it begins to quiver and swell, putting forth every fine variety (of herbage).

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: O People, if you should be in doubt about the Resurrection, then [consider that] indeed, We created you from dust, then from a sperm-drop, then from a clinging clot, and then from a lump of flesh, formed and unformed - that We may show you. And We settle in the wombs whom We will for a specified term, then We bring you out as a child, and then [We develop you] that you may reach your [time of] maturity. And among you is he who is taken in [early] death, and among you is he who is returned to the most decrepit [old] age so that he knows, after [once having] knowledge, nothing. And you see the earth barren, but when We send down upon it rain, it quivers and swells and grows [something] of every beautiful kind.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: People, if you have doubts about the Resurrection, you must know that We created you from clay that was turned into a living germ. This was developed into a clot of blood, which was made into a well formed and partly shapeless lump of flesh. This is how We show you that resurrection is not more difficult for Us than your creation. We cause whatever We want to stay in the womb for an appointed time, We then take you out of the womb as a baby, so that you may grow up and receive strength. Some of you may then die and others may grow to a very old age and lose your memory. You may see the earth as a barren land, but when we send rain, it starts to stir and swell and produce various pairs of attractive herbs.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: O people! if you are in doubt about the raising, then surely We created you from dust, then from a small seed, then from a clot, then from a lump of flesh, complete in make and incomplete, that We may make clear to you; and We cause what We please to stay in the wombs till an appointed time, then We bring you forth as babies, then that you may attain your maturity; and of you is he who is caused to die, and of you is he who is brought back to the worst part of life, so that after having knowledge he does not know anything; and you see the earth sterile land, but when We send down on it the water, it stirs and swells and brings forth of every kind a beautiful herbage.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: O people! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection, remember that We first created you from dust, then from a sperm drop, then from clotted blood, then a lump of flesh, both shaped and unshaped, so that We might manifest to you [Our power]. We cause what We will to stay in the womb for an appointed time, then We bring you forth as infants and then We cause you to grow and reach full growth. Then, some of you will pass away early in life, while some of you will reach extreme old age in which they will know nothing of what they once knew. You see the earth, dead and barren, but no sooner do We send down rain upon it than it begins to stir and swell, and produce every kind of luxuriant vegetation:

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: O mankind! if ye have a doubt about the Resurrection, (consider) that We created you out of dust, then out of sperm, then out of a leech-like clot, then out of a morsel of flesh, partly formed and partly unformed, in order that We may manifest (our power) to you; and We cause whom We will to rest in the wombs for an appointed term, then do We bring you out as babes, then (foster you) that ye may reach your age of full strength; and some of you are called to die, and some are sent back to the feeblest old age, so that they know nothing after having known (much), and (further), thou seest the earth barren and lifeless, but when We pour down rain on it, it is stirred (to life), it swells, and it puts forth every kind of beautiful growth (in pairs).

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Ya ayyuha alnnasu in kuntum fee raybin mina albaAAthi fainna khalaqnakum min turabin thumma min nutfatin thumma min AAalaqatin thumma min mudghatin mukhallaqatin waghayri mukhallaqatin linubayyina lakum wanuqirru fee alarhami ma nashao ila ajalin musamman thumma nukhrijukum tiflan thumma litablughoo ashuddakum waminkum man yutawaffa waminkum man yuraddu ila arthali alAAumuri likayla yaAAlama min baAAdi AAilmin shayan watara alarda hamidatan faitha anzalna AAalayha almaa ihtazzat warabat waanbatat min kulli zawjin baheejin

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: That is so for God is the undeniable Reality. It is He who brings the dead to life, for He has power over everything.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: This is because Allah only is True and because He will revive the dead, and because He is Able to do all things.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: That is because God -- He is the Truth, and brings the dead to life, and is powerful over everything,

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: That, because Allah! He is The Truth, and He quickeneth the dead, and verily He is over everything Potent.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: That is because Allah, He is the Truth, and it is He Who gives life to the dead, and it is He Who is Able to do all things.

6.author: Itani
6.text: That is because God is the truth, and because He gives life to the dead, and because He is Capable of everything.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: All this is because Allah, He is the Truth, and because He resurrects the dead, and because He has power over everything,

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: That is because Allah: He is the Truth, and it is He Who gives life to the dead, and it is He Who is able to do all things.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: That is because Allah, He is the Truth and because He quickeneth the dead, and because He is Able to do all things;

6.author: Qarai
6.text: All that is because Allah is the Reality and it is He who revives the dead, and He has power over all things,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: That is because Allah is the Truth; He revives the dead and has power over all things.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: That is because Allah is the Truth and because He gives life to the dead and because He is over all things competent

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: This is because God is the Supreme Truth who gives life to the dead and who has power over all things.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: This is because Allah is the Truth and because He gives life to the dead and because He has power over all things

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: that is because God is the truth. It is He who gives life to the dead and He has the power to will anything.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: This is so, because Allah is the Reality: it is He Who gives life to the dead, and it is He Who has power over all things.

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Thalika bianna Allaha huwa alhaqqu waannahu yuhyee almawta waannahu AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: The Hour will come without a doubt, and God will raise those who are dead.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And because the Last Day will come, there is no doubt in it – and because Allah will revive those who are in the graves.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: and because the Hour is coming, no doubt of it, and God shall raise up whosoever is within the tombs.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And because the Hour is coming- there is no doubt thereof-- and because Allah will raise up those who are in the graves.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And surely, the Hour is coming, there is no doubt about it, and certainly, Allah will resurrect those who are in the graves.

7.author: Itani
7.text: And because the Hour is coming—there is no doubt about it—and because God will resurrect those in the graves.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: (all of which shows that) the Hour shall surely come to pass - in this there is no doubt - and Allah shall surely resurrect those that are in graves.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And surely, the Hour is coming, there is no doubt about it; and certainly, Allah will resurrect those who are in the graves.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And because the Hour will come, there is no doubt thereof; and because Allah will raise those who are in the graves.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: and because the Hour is bound to come, there is no doubt in it, and Allah will resurrect those who are in the graves.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: And the Hour is sure to come there is no doubt. And Allah will raise up those in the graves.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And [that they may know] that the Hour is coming - no doubt about it - and that Allah will resurrect those in the graves.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: There is no doubt about the coming of the Hour of Doom and that God will raise every one from their graves.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And because the hour is coming, there is no doubt about it; and because Allah shall raise up those who are in the graves.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: The Last Hour is bound to come. There is no doubt about it. God will raise up those who are in their graves.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: And verily the Hour will come: there can be no doubt about it, or about (the fact) that Allah will raise up all who are in the graves.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Waanna alssaAAata atiyatun la rayba feeha waanna Allaha yabAAathu man fee alquboori

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Yet there are some who contend about God without any knowledge or guidance or enlightening Book,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And there is one who argues concerning Allah without having knowledge nor any proof nor a clear text.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: And among men there is such a one that disputes concerning God without knowledge or guidance, or an illuminating Book,

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And of mankind is he who disputeth respecting Allah without knowledge or guidance or a Book luminous.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And among men is he who disputes about Allah, without knowledge or guidance, or a Book giving light (from Allah),

8.author: Itani
8.text: And among the people is he who argues about God without knowledge, or guidance, or an enlightening scripture.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: And among people are those that wrangle about Allah without knowledge, without any true guidance, and without any scripture to enlighten them.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And among men is he who disputes about Allah, without knowledge or guidance, or a Book giving light (from Allah).

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: And among mankind is he who disputeth concerning Allah without knowledge or guidance or a scripture giving light,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Among the people are those who dispute concerning Allah without any knowledge or guidance, or an enlightening Book,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Some dispute about Allah, though they have neither knowledge nor guidance, nor an illuminating Book.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And of the people is he who disputes about Allah without knowledge or guidance or an enlightening book [from Him],

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Some people argue about God without knowledge, guidance, and an enlightening Book.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And among men there is he who disputes about Allah without knowledge and without guidance and without an illuminating book,

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: There are some who dispute about God without having any knowledge or guidance, or any enlightening Book.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Yet there is among men such a one as disputes about Allah, without Knowledge, without Guidance, and without a Book of Enlightenment,-

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Wamina alnnasi man yujadilu fee Allahi bighayri AAilmin wala hudan wala kitabin muneerin

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Turning their backs that they may lead away from the path of God. For such there is disgrace in the world, and on the Day of Judgement We shall make them taste the torment of burning.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: With his neck turned away from the truth, in order to deceive from the way of Allah; for him is disgrace in this world and on the Day of Resurrection We shall make him taste the punishment of fire.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: turning his side to lead astray from God's way; for him is degradation in this world; and on the Resurrection Day We shall let him taste the chastisement of the burning:

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Bending his neck, that he may lead astray from the way of Allah; his shall be humiliation in the world, and We shall make him taste on the Day of Judgment the torment of Burning.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Bending his neck in pride (far astray from the Path of Allah), and leading (others) too (far) astray from the Path of Allah. For him there is disgrace in this worldly life, and on the Day of Resurrection We shall make him taste the torment of burning (Fire).

9.author: Itani
9.text: Turning aside in contempt, to lead away from the path of God. He will have humiliation in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection We will make him taste the agony of burning.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: They wrangle arrogantly, intent on leading people astray from the Way of Allah. Such shall suffer disgrace in this world and We shall cause them to taste the chastisement of burning (in the Next).

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Bending his neck in pride, and leading (others) too (far) astray from the path of Allah. For him there is disgrace in this worldly life, and on the Day of Resurrection We shall make him taste the torment of burning.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Turning away in pride to beguile (men) from the way of Allah. For him in this world is ignominy, and on the Day of Resurrection We make him taste the doom of burning.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: turning aside disdainfully to lead [others] astray from the way of Allah. For such there is disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection We will make him taste the punishment of the burning:

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: There is for him who turns his side to lead (others) astray from the Path of Allah, degradation in this life and We shall let him taste the punishment of theburning on the Day of Resurrection.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: Twisting his neck [in arrogance] to mislead [people] from the way of Allah. For him in the world is disgrace, and We will make him taste on the Day of Resurrection the punishment of the Burning Fire [while it is said],

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: They turn away (from the Truth) to lead people away from the path of God. Their share in this world will be disgrace and on the Day of Judgment a burning torment.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Turning away haughtily that he may lead (others) astray from the way of Allah; for him is disgrace in this world, and on the day of resurrection We will make him taste the punishment of burning:

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: They turn away arrogantly, leading people astray from God's path. Such men shall incur disgrace in this life and taste the punishment of the Fire on the Day of Judgement.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: (Disdainfully) bending his side, in order to lead (men) astray from the Path of Allah: for him there is disgrace in this life, and on the Day of Judgment We shall make him taste the Penalty of burning (Fire).

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Thaniya AAitfihi liyudilla AAan sabeeli Allahi lahu fee alddunya khizyun wanutheequhu yawma alqiyamati AAathaba alhareeqi

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: That is on account of what you had done in the past; yet God is not unjust to His creatures.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: “This is the recompense of what your hands have sent ahead, and Allah does not oppress His bondmen."

10.author: Arberry
10.text: 'That is for what thy hands have forwarded and for that God is never unjust unto His servants.'

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: That is because of that which thy hands have sent forth, and verily Allah is not a wronger of His bondmen.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: That is because of what your hands have sent forth, and verily, Allah is not unjust to (His) slaves.

10.author: Itani
10.text: That is for what your hands have advanced, and because God is not unjust to the servants.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: That is the outcome of what your own hands have wrought, for Allah never wrongs His creatures.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: That is because of what your hands have sent forth, and verily, Allah is not unjust to servants.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: (And unto him it will be said): This is for that which thy two hands have sent before, and because Allah is no oppressor of His slaves.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: ‘That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not tyrannical to the servants.’

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: 'This, is the reward for what your hands have forwarded. Allah is not unjust to the worshipers'

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: "That is for what your hands have put forth and because Allah is not ever unjust to [His] servants."

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: (They will be told), "This is the result of what your hands have wrought. God is certainly not unjust to His servants."

10.author: Shakir
10.text: This is due to what your two hands have sent before, and because Allah is not in the least unjust to the servants.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: [God will say],"This is the reward of your misdeeds. God is not unjust to His servants."

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: (It will be said): "This is because of the deeds which thy hands sent forth, for verily Allah is not unjust to His servants.

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Thalika bima qaddamat yadaka waanna Allaha laysa bithallamin lilAAabeedi

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: There are some men who worship God only from the margin. If there is some profit they are content; but if calamity befalls them they turn about, thus losing both this world and the next. This is indeed a palpable loss.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: And there are some men who worship Allah upon an edge; then if some good occurs to them, they are content; and if some trial comes, they turn way upon their faces; a loss of this world and the Hereafter; and this is the complete loss.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: And among men there is such a one as serves God upon the very edge -- if good befalls him he is at rest in it, but if a trial befalls him he turns completely over; he loses this world and the world to come; that is indeed the manifest loss.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And of mankind is he who worshippeth Allah as on edge; if there befalleth him good, he in contented therewith, and if there befalleth him a trial, he turneth round on his face; he loseth the world and the Hereafter: that indeed is a loss manifest!

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: And among mankind is he who worships Allah as it were, upon the very edge (i.e. in doubt); if good befalls him, he is content therewith; but if a trial befalls him, he turns back on his face (i.e. reverts back to disbelief after embracing Islam). He loses both this world and the Hereafter. That is the evident loss.

11.author: Itani
11.text: And among the people is he who worships God on edge. When something good comes his way, he is content with it. But when an ordeal strikes him, he makes a turnaround. He loses this world and the next. That is the obvious loss.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: And among people is he who worships Allah on the borderline; if any good befalls him, he is satisfied; but if a trial afflicts him, he utterly turns away. He will incur the loss of this world and the Hereafter. That indeed is a clear loss.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: And among mankind is he who worships Allah as it were upon the edge: if good befalls him, he is content therewith; but if a Fitnah strikes him, he turns back on his face. He loses both this world and the Hereafter. That is the evident loss.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: And among mankind is he who worshippeth Allah upon a narrow marge so that if good befalleth him he is content therewith, but if a trial befalleth him, he falleth away utterly. He loseth both the world and the Hereafter. That is the sheer loss.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: And among the people are those who worship Allah on the [very] fringe: if good fortune befalls him, he is content with it; but if an ordeal visits him he makes a turnabout, to become a loser in the world and the Hereafter. That is manifest loss.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: There are among the people such who worship Allah and (yet stand) on the very edge. When goodness comes to him, he is content, but if a trial befalls him he falls upon his face, he loses this world and the Everlasting Life; that is indeed a clear loss.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And of the people is he who worships Allah on an edge. If he is touched by good, he is reassured by it; but if he is struck by trial, he turns on his face [to the other direction]. He has lost [this] world and the Hereafter. That is what is the manifest loss.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: Some people worship God to achieve worldly gains. They are confident when they are prosperous, but when they face hardships they turn away from (worship). They are lost in this life and will be lost in the life to come. Such loss is indeed destructive.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And among men is he who serves Allah (standing) on the verge, so that if good befalls him he is satisfied therewith, but if a trial afflict him he turns back headlong; he loses this world as well as the hereafter; that is a manifest loss.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: There are some who worship God half-heartedly, then, if some good befalls them, they are content with it, but if an ordeal befalls them, they revert to their former ways. They lose in this world as well in the Hereafter. That is a clear loss.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: There are among men some who serve Allah, as it were, on the verge: if good befalls them, they are, therewith, well content; but if a trial comes to them, they turn on their faces: they lose both this world and the Hereafter: that is loss for all to see!

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Wamina alnnasi man yaAAbudu Allaha AAala harfin fain asabahu khayrun itmaanna bihi wain asabathu fitnatun inqalaba AAala wajhihi khasira alddunya waalakhirata thalika huwa alkhusranu almubeenu

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: Leaving God they pray to those who cannot harm or profit them. That is the limit of going astray.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: They worship such, beside Allah, which neither harms them nor benefits them; this only is the extreme error.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: He calls, apart from God, upon that which hurts him not, and which neither profits him anything; that is indeed the far error.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: He calleth upon that, beside Allah, which can neither hurt him nor profit him! that! it is a straying far-off.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: He calls besides Allah unto that which hurts him not, nor profits him. That is a straying far away.

12.author: Itani
12.text: He invokes, instead of God, what can neither harm him nor benefit him. That is the far straying.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: He invokes, instead of Allah, those who can neither harm nor benefit him. That indeed is straying far away.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: He calls besides Allah unto that which can neither harm him nor profit him. That is a straying far away.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: He calleth, beside Allah, unto that which hurteth him not nor benefiteth him. That is the far error.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: He invokes besides Allah that which can bring him neither benefit nor harm. That is extreme error.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: He calls to other than Allah, to that which neither hurts him, nor benefits him; that is indeed far error.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: He invokes instead of Allah that which neither harms him nor benefits him. That is what is the extreme error.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: They worship things instead of God which can neither harm them nor benefit them. This is indeed to stray far away from the right path.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: He calls besides Allah upon that which does not harm him and that which does not profit him, that is the great straying.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: He calls on, instead of God, something that can neither harm him, nor benefit him. That is indeed straying far away --

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: They call on such deities, besides Allah, as can neither hurt nor profit them: that is straying far indeed (from the Way)!

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: YadAAoo min dooni Allahi ma la yadurruhu wama la yanfaAAuhu thalika huwa alddalalu albaAAeedu

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: They pray to him whose bane is more imminent than his boon: How bad the protector and how bad the associate!

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: They worship one from whom harm is expected more than the benefit; indeed what an evil master and indeed what an evil friend!

13.author: Arberry
13.text: He calls upon him who is likelier to hurt him, rather than to profit him -- an evil protector indeed, he, an evil friend!

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: He calleth upon him whose hurt is nearer than his profit: surely ill the patron! ill the comrade!

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: He calls unto him whose harm is nearer than his profit; certainly, and evil Maula (patron) and certainly an evil friend!

13.author: Itani
13.text: He invokes one whose harm is closer than his benefit. What a miserable master. What a miserable companion.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: He invokes those that are more likely to cause him harm than benefit. Such is surely an evil patron, and an evil associate.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: He calls unto him whose harm is nearer than his profit; certainly an evil Mawla and certainly an evil `Ashir!

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: He calleth unto him whose harm is nearer than his benefit; verily an evil patron and verily an evil friend!

13.author: Qarai
13.text: He invokes someone whose harm is surely likelier than his benefit. An evil ally indeed, and an evil companion!

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: He calls upon him whose harm is nearer than his benefit, an evil guide and an evil friend.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: He invokes one whose harm is closer than his benefit - how wretched the protector and how wretched the associate.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: Their worship of such things, in which there is no hope for any benefit, can only harm them. How terrible is such a guardian and companion!

13.author: Shakir
13.text: He calls upon him whose harm is nearer than his profit; evil certainly is the guardian and evil certainly is the associate.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: he calls on that which would sooner harm than help. Such a patron is indeed evil and such a companion is indeed evil.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: (Perhaps) they call on one whose hurt is nearer than his profit: evil, indeed, is the patron, and evil the companion (or help)!

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: YadAAoo laman darruhu aqrabu min nafAAihi labisa almawla walabisa alAAasheeru

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: God will admit those who believe and do the right to gardens full of rippling streams. Verily God does as He pleases.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: Indeed Allah will admit those who believed and did good deeds, into Gardens beneath which rivers flow; indeed Allah does whatever He wills.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: God shall surely admit those who believe and do righteous deeds into gardens underneath which rivers flow; surely God does that He desires.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Verily Allah shall cause those who believe and work righteous works to enter Gardens whereunder rivers flow; verily Allah doth whatsoever He intendeth.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Truly, Allah will admit those who believe (in Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds (according to the Quran and the Sunnah) to Gardens underneath which rivers flow (in Paradise). Verily, Allah does what He wills.

14.author: Itani
14.text: God will admit those who believe and do righteous deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. God does whatever He wills.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: (In contrast) Allah will assuredly cause those who believe and act righteously to enter Gardens beneath which rivers flow. For, most certainly, Allah does whatever He pleases.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Truly, Allah will admit those who believe and do righteous good deeds to Gardens underneath which rivers flow. Verily, Allah does what He wills.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Lo! Allah causeth those who believe and do good works to enter Gardens underneath which rivers flow. Lo! Allah doth what He intendeth.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: Allah will indeed admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them. Indeed Allah does whatever He desires.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: As for those who believe and do good works Allah will admit them to gardens underneath which rivers flow. Allah indeed does what He will.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: Indeed, Allah will admit those who believe and do righteous deeds to gardens beneath which rivers flow. Indeed, Allah does what He intends.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: God will admit the righteously striving believers to the gardens wherein streams flow. God has all the power to do whatever He wants.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Surely Allah will cause those who believe and do good deeds to enter gardens beneath which rivers flow, surely Allah does what He pleases.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: God will admit those who believe and act righteously into Gardens watered by flowing rivers. God does whatever He wills.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Verily Allah will admit those who believe and work righteous deeds, to Gardens, beneath which rivers flow: for Allah carries out all that He plans.

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Inna Allaha yudkhilu allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati jannatin tajree min tahtiha alanharu inna Allaha yafAAalu ma yureedu

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: He who thinks that God will not help him in this world and the next should stretch a rope to the sky then cut it off and see if his mind is relieved (of doubts) by this stratagem.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Therefore whoever assumes that Allah will not assist His Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) in this world and the Hereafter, should extend a rope upwards and hang himself, and then see whether his scheme has taken away what he envies.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Whosoever thinks God will not help him in the present world and the world to come; let him stretch up a rope to heaven, then let him sever it, and behold whether his guile does away with what enrages him.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Whosoever hath been imagining that Allah shall not make him victorious in the world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a cord up to the heaven and let him cut it, and let him look if his stratagem can do away that whereat he enrageth.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Whoever thinks that Allah will not help him (Muhammad SAW) in this world and in the Hereafter, let him stretch out a rope to the ceiling and let him strangle himself. Then let him see whether his plan will remove that whereat he rages!

15.author: Itani
15.text: Whoever thinks that God will not help him in this life and in the Hereafter—let him turn to heaven, then sever, and see if his cunning eliminates what enrages him.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Anyone who fancies that Allah will not support him in this world and in the Hereafter, let him reach out to heaven through a rope, and then make a hole in the sky and see whether his device can avert that which enrages him.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Whoever thinks that Allah will not help him in this world and in the Hereafter, let him stretch out a rope to the ceiling and let him strangle himself. Then let him see whether his plan will remove that whereat he rages!

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Whoso is wont to think (through envy) that Allah will not give him (Muhammad) victory in the world and the Hereafter (and is enraged at the thought of his victory), let him stretch a rope up to the roof (of his dwelling), and let him hang himself. Then let him see whether his strategy dispelleth that whereat he rageth!.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Whoever thinks that Allah will not help him in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the ceiling and hang himself, and see if his artifice would remove his rage.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: If any one thinks that Allah will not give him (Prophet Muhammad) victory in this present world and in the Everlasting Life, let him stretch a rope to heaven, and let him sever it. Then, let him see if his guile does away with that which has enraged him.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Whoever should think that Allah will not support [Prophet Muhammad] in this world and the Hereafter - let him extend a rope to the ceiling, then cut off [his breath], and let him see: will his effort remove that which enrages [him]?

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: Those who thought that God would never grant victory to (Muhammad), in this world nor in the life hereafter (and now that he is victorious) should hang themselves by the necks from the ceiling, then cut the rope and see if this can do away with what has enraged them.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Whoever thinks that Allah will not assist him in this life and the hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the ceiling, then let him cut (it) off, then let him see if his struggle will take away that at which he is enraged.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Anyone who thinks that God will not help him [His messenger] in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope up to the sky; then let him cut it off and see if his plan can help to remove the cause of his anger.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: If any think that Allah will not help him (His Messenger) in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch out a rope to the ceiling and cut (himself) off: then let him see whether his plan will remove that which enrages (him)!

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Man kana yathunnu an lan yansurahu Allahu fee alddunya waalakhirati falyamdud bisababin ila alssamai thumma liyaqtaAA falyanthur hal yuthhibanna kayduhu ma yagheethu

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: That is why We have sent down these clear revelations, for God gives guidance whomsoever He please.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And so it is, that We sent down this Qur’an – clear verses – and that Allah guides whomever He wills.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: Even so We have sent it down as signs, clear signs, and for that God guides whom He desires.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And Thus We have sent it down as evidences, and verily Allah guideth whomsoever He intendeth.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Thus have We sent it (this Quran) down (to Muhammad SAW) as clear signs, evidences and proofs, and surely, Allah guides whom He wills.

16.author: Itani
16.text: Thus We revealed it as clarifying signs, and God guides whomever He wills.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: Even so We have revealed the Qur'an with Clear Signs. Verily Allah guides whomsoever He wills.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: Thus have We sent it down as clear Ayat, and surely, Allah guides whom He wills.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Thus We reveal it as plain revelations, and verily Allah guideth whom He will.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: Thus have We sent it down as manifest signs, and indeed Allah guides whomever He desires.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: As such We have sent it in clear verses. Indeed, Allah gives guidance to whom He will.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And thus have We sent the Qur'an down as verses of clear evidence and because Allah guides whom He intends.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: We have revealed the Quran which contains authoritative verses. God guides only those whom He wants.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And thus have We revealed it, being clear arguments, and because Allah guides whom He intends.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: We have sent down the Quran as clear evidence, and surely God guides whom He will.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Thus have We sent down Clear Signs; and verily Allah doth guide whom He will!

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Wakathalika anzalnahu ayatin bayyinatin waanna Allaha yahdee man yureedu

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: God will judge between those who believe and the Jews, the Sabians, Christians and the Magians and the idolaters, on the Day of Judgement. Verily God is witness to everything.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: Indeed the Muslims, and the Jews, and the Sabeans, and the Christians and the fire worshippers and the polytheists – indeed Allah will decide between all of them on the Day of Resurrection; indeed Allah witnesses all things.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Surely they that believe, and those of Jewry, the Sabaeans, the Christians, the Magians and the idolaters -- God shall distinguish between them on the Day of Resurrection; assuredly God is witness over everything.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Verily those who believe and those who are Judaised and the Sabians and the Nazarenes and the Magians and those who associate - verily Allah will decide between them on the Day of Judgment; verily Allah is over everything a Witness.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Verily, those who believe (in Allah and in His Messenger Muhammad SAW), and those who are Jews, and the Sabians, and the Christians, and the Magians, and those who worship others besides Allah, truly, Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. Verily! Allah is Witness over all things.

17.author: Itani
17.text: Those who believe, and those who are Jewish, and the Sabeans, and the Christians, and the Zoroastrians, and the Polytheists—God will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. God is witness to all things.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: On the Day of Resurrection Allah will most certainly judge among those who believe, and those who became Jews, and Sabaeans, and Christians, and Magians, and those who associate others with Allah in His Divinity. Surely Allah watches over everything.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Verily, those who believe, and those who are Jews, and the Sabians, and the Christians, and the Majus, and those who worship others besides Allah; truly, Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. Verily, Allah is over all things a Witness.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Lo! those who believe (this revelation), and those who are Jews, and the Sabaeans and the Christians and the Magians and the idolaters - Lo! Allah will decide between them on the Day of Resurrection. Lo! Allah is Witness over all things.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Indeed Allah will indeed judge between the faithful, the Jews, the Sabaeans, the Christians, the Magians and the polytheists on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed Allah is witness to all things.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: Surely, they that believe, and those of Jewry, the Sabaeans, the Nazarenes, the Magians, and the unbelievers, Allah will judge them on the Day of Resurrection. Surely, Allah is witness over everything.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Indeed, those who have believed and those who were Jews and the Sabeans and the Christians and the Magians and those who associated with Allah - Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed Allah is, over all things, Witness.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: On the Day of Judgment, God will make truth and falsehood clearly distinct from each other to the believers, the Jews, the Sabeans, the Christian, the Zoroastrians, and the Pagans on the Day of Judgment. God is a Witness to all things.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Surely those who believe and those who are Jews and the Sabeans and the Christians and the Magians and those who associate (others with Allah)-- surely Allah will decide between them on the day of resurrection; surely Allah is a witness over all things.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: God will judge between the believers, the Jews, the Sabaeans, the Christians, the Magians and the polytheists on the Day of Judgement. Surely God is witness to everything.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Those who believe (in the Qur'an), those who follow the Jewish (scriptures), and the Sabians, Christians, Magians, and Polytheists,- Allah will judge between them on the Day of Judgment: for Allah is witness of all things.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Inna allatheena amanoo waallatheena hadoo waalssabieena waalnnasara waalmajoosa waallatheena ashrakoo inna Allaha yafsilu baynahum yawma alqiyamati inna Allaha AAala kulli shayin shaheedun

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: Do you see how all things in heavens and the earth, the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, trees and beasts, and men in abundance, pay homage to God? Yet there are many who deserve the punishment. And whosoever God disgraces will have none to raise him up in honour. God does verily as He will.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Did you not see that for Allah prostrate those who are in the heavens and in the earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the hills, and the trees, and the beasts, and many among mankind; and there are many upon whom the punishment has been decreed; and he whom Allah disgraces – there is none to give him honour; indeed Allah may do whatever He wills. (Command of Prostration # 6)

18.author: Arberry
18.text: Hast thou not seen how to God bow all who are in the and all who are in the earth, heavens the sun and the moon, the stars and the mountains, the trees and the beasts, and many of mankind? And many merit the chastisement; and whom God abases, there is none to honour him. God does whatsoever He will.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Beholdest thou not that Allah unto Him adore whosoever is in the heavens and on the earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the mountains, and the trees and the beasts, and many of mankind! And many are there on whom due is the torment. And whomsoever Allah despiseth, none can honour; verily Allah doth whatsoever He willeth.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: See you not that to Allah prostrates whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the mountains, and the trees, and Ad-Dawab (moving living creatures, beasts, etc.), and many of mankind? But there are many (men) on whom the punishment is justified. And whomsoever Allah disgraces, none can honour him. Verily! Allah does what He wills.

18.author: Itani
18.text: Do you not realize that to God prostrates everyone in the heavens and everyone on earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the mountains, and the trees, and the animals, and many of the people? But many are justly deserving of punishment. Whomever God shames, there is none to honor him. God does whatever He wills.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: Have you not seen that all those who are in the heavens and all those who are in the earth prostrate themselves before Allah; and so do the sun and the moon, and the stars and the mountains, and the trees, and the beasts, and so do many human beings, and even many of those who are condemned to chastisement? And he whom Allah humiliates, none can give him honour. Allah does whatever He wills.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: See you not that whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the mountains, and the trees, and Ad-Dawabb, and many of mankind prostrate themselves to Allah. But there are many (men) on whom the punishment is justified. And whomsoever Allah disgraces, none can honor him. Verily, Allah does what He wills.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Hast thou not seen that unto Allah payeth adoration whosoever is in the heavens and whosoever is in the earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the hills, and the trees, and the beasts, and many of mankind, while there are many unto whom the doom is justly due. He whom Allah scorneth, there is none to give him honour. Lo! Allah doeth what He will.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: Have you not regarded that whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth prostrates to Allah, as well as the sun, the moon, and the stars, the mountains, the trees, and the animals and many humans? And many have come to deserve the punishment. Whomever Allah humiliates will find no one who may bring him honour. Indeed Allah does whatever He wishes.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: Have you not seen that to Allah prostrate all who are in the heavens and all who are in the earth, the sun and the moon, the stars and the mountains, the trees and the beasts, and many people? And many deserve the punishment. He who is abased by Allah has none to honor him. Allah does what He will.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Do you not see that to Allah prostrates whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth and the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, the moving creatures and many of the people? But upon many the punishment has been justified. And he whom Allah humiliates - for him there is no bestower of honor. Indeed, Allah does what He wills.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: Have you not considered that those in the heavens and the earth, the Sun, the Moon, the Stars, the mountains, the trees, the animals, and many people, all bow down to God? But many people deserve His torment. No one can give honor to whomever God has insulted. God has all the power to do what He wants.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Do you not see that Allah is He, Whom obeys whoever is in the heavens and whoever is in the earth, and the sun and the moon and the stars, and the mountains and the trees, and the animals and many of the people; and many there are against whom chastisement has become necessary; and whomsoever Allah abases, there is none who can make him honorable; surely Allah does what He pleases.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: Do you not see that whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth, as well as the sun and the moon, and the stars and the mountains, and the trees and the beasts and many human beings -- all submit to God? But there are many who have become deserving of punishment. Whoever God disgraces, will have no one to honour him. Surely, God does what He wills.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Seest thou not that to Allah bow down in worship all things that are in the heavens and on earth,- the sun, the moon, the stars; the hills, the trees, the animals; and a great number among mankind? But a great number are (also) such as are fit for Punishment: and such as Allah shall disgrace,- None can raise to honour: for Allah carries out all that He wills.

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Alam tara anna Allaha yasjudu lahu man fee alssamawati waman fee alardi waalshshamsu waalqamaru waalnnujoomu waaljibalu waalshshajaru waalddawabbu wakatheerun mina alnnasi wakatheerun haqqa AAalayhi alAAathabu waman yuhini Allahu fama lahu min mukrimin inna Allaha yafAAalu ma yashao

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: These two (believers and unbelievers) are disputants, who contend about their Lord. But they who disbelieve will be fitted out with garments of flames. Boiling water will be poured down over their heads

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: These are the two groups who fought concerning their Lord; so those who disbelieved – garments of fire have been fashioned for them; and boiling water will be poured onto their heads.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: These are two disputants who have disputed concerning their Lord. As for the unbelievers, for them garments of fire shall, be cut, and there shall be poured over their heads boiling water

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: These twain are opponents who contended respecting their Lord; then as for those who disbelieved, cut out for them shall be raiments of frre: poured out over their head shall be hot water.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: These two opponents (believers and disbelievers) dispute with each other about their Lord; then as for those who disbelieve, garments of fire will be cut out for them, boiling water will be poured down over their heads.

19.author: Itani
19.text: Here are two adversaries feuding regarding their Lord. As for those who disbelieve, garments of fire will be tailored for them, and scalding water will be poured over their heads.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: These two groups (the believers and unbelievers) are in dispute about their Lord. As for those that disbelieve, garments of fire have been cut out for them; boiling water shall be poured down over their heads,

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: These two opponents dispute with each other about their Lord; then as for those who disbelieved, garments of fire will be cut out for them, boiling water will be poured down over their heads.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: These twain (the believers and the disbelievers) are two opponents who contend concerning their Lord. But as for those who disbelieve, garments of fire will be cut out for them; boiling fluid will be poured down on their heads,

19.author: Qarai
19.text: These two contending groups contend concerning their Lord. As for those who are faithless, cloaks of fire will be cut out for them, and boiling water will be poured over their heads,

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Those are two who disputed concerning their Lord. Garments of fire have been prepared for the unbelievers. Boiling water shall be poured over their heads,

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: These are two adversaries who have disputed over their Lord. But those who disbelieved will have cut out for them garments of fire. Poured upon their heads will be scalding water

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: (Those who prostrate themselves before God and those who do not) are two groups who dispute with each other about their Lord. For the unbelievers the garment of fire has already been prepared. Boiling water will be poured upon their heads.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: These are two adversaries who dispute about their Lord; then (as to) those who disbelieve, for them are cut out garments of fire, boiling water shall be poured over their heads.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: These two groups, [the believers and those who deny the truth], dispute concerning their Lord. Those who deny the truth will have garments of fire cut out for them; and boiling water will be poured down over their heads,

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: These two antagonists dispute with each other about their Lord: But those who deny (their Lord),- for them will be cut out a garment of Fire: over their heads will be poured out boiling water.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Hathani khasmani ikhtasamoo fee rabbihim faallatheena kafaroo quttiAAat lahum thiyabun min narin yusabbu min fawqi ruoosihimu alhameemu

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Which will dissolve everything within their bellies, and their skins.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: With which will melt what is in their bellies, and their skins.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: whereby whatsoever is in their bellies and their skins shall be melted;

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Molten thereby shall be that which is in their bellies and also their skins.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: With it will melt or vanish away what is within their bellies, as well as (their) skins.

20.author: Itani
20.text: Melting their insides and their skins.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: causing (not only) their skins but all that is in their bellies as well to melt away.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: With it will melt (or vanish away) what is within their bellies, as well as (their) skins.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Whereby that which is in their bellies, and their skins too, will be melted;

20.author: Qarai
20.text: with which their skins and entrails will fuse,

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: and that which is in their bellies and their skins shall be melted;

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: By which is melted that within their bellies and [their] skins.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: It will melt their skins and all that is in their bellies.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: With it shall be melted what is in their bellies and (their) skins as well.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: anything in their stomachs as well as their skins will be melted by it.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: With it will be scalded what is within their bodies, as well as (their) skins.

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Yusharu bihi ma fee butoonihim waaljuloodu

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: There are iron maces for them.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And for them are rods of iron.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: for them await hooked iron rods;

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And for them shall be maces of iron.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: And for them are hooked rods of iron (to punish them).

21.author: Itani
21.text: And they will have maces of iron.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: There shall be maces of iron to lash them.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: And for them are hooked rods of iron.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: And for them are hooked rods of iron.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: and there will be clubs of iron for them.

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: for them are hooked rods of iron.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And for [striking] them are maces of iron.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: They will be subdued by iron rods.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And for them are whips of iron.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: There will be maces of iron for them;

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: In addition there will be maces of iron (to punish) them.

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: As often as they try to escape from its anguish they would be put back into (the fire), and taste the torment of burning.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: Whenever they wish to come out of it due to suffocation, they will be returned to it again and it will be commanded, “Taste the punishment of fire!”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: as often as they desire in their anguish to come forth from it, they shall be restored into it, and: 'Taste the chastisement of the burning!'

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: So oft as they would seek to go forth therefrom, because of anguish, they shall be sent back therein, and taste the torment of Burning.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: Every time they seek to get away therefrom, from anguish, they will be driven back therein, and (it will be) said to them: "Taste the torment of burning!"

22.author: Itani
22.text: Whenever they try to escape the gloom, they will be driven back to it: “Taste the suffering of burning.”

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Whenever they try, in their anguish, to escape from Hell, they will be driven back into it, (and shall be told): "Now taste the torment of burning."

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: Every time they seek to get away therefrom, from anguish, they will be driven back therein, and (it will be) said to them: "Taste the torment of burning!"

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: Whenever, in their anguish, they would go forth from thence they are driven back therein and (it is said unto them): Taste the doom of burning.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Whenever they desire to leave it out of anguish, they will be turned back into it [and told]: ‘Taste the punishment of the burning!’

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: Whenever in their anguish they try to get out of it, they are restored to it. (It will be said): 'Taste the punishment of burning'

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: Every time they want to get out of Hellfire from anguish, they will be returned to it, and [it will be said], "Taste the punishment of the Burning Fire!"

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: Whenever in anguish they will try to come out of hell they will be returned therein to suffer the burning torment.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: Whenever they will desire to go forth from it, from grief, they shall be turned back into it, and taste the chastisement of burning.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: whenever, in their anguish they seek to escape from Hell, they will be driven back into it, and they will be told, "Taste the punishment of Hell."

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Every time they wish to get away therefrom, from anguish, they will be forced back therein, and (it will be said), "Taste ye the Penalty of Burning!"

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Kullama aradoo an yakhrujoo minha min ghammin oAAeedoo feeha wathooqoo AAathaba alhareeqi

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: God will surely admit those who believe and do the right to gardens with rivers running by, where they will be decked in bracelets of gold and of pearls; and of silk will be their garments.

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Indeed Allah will admit those who believed and did good deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow – in it they will be made to wear armlets of gold, and pearls, and in it their garment is silk.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: God shall surely admit those who believe and do righteous deeds into gardens underneath which rivers flow; therein they shall be adorned with bracelets of gold and with pearls, and their apparel there shall be of silk;

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Verily Allah will cause those who believe and work righteous works to enter Gardens whereunder the rivers flow, wherein they will be bedecked with brace lets of gold and with pearls, and their garment therein shall be of silk.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Truly, Allah will admit those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, to Gardens underneath which rivers flow (in Paradise), wherein they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls and their garments therein will be of silk.

23.author: Itani
23.text: But God will admit those who believe and do good deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. They will be decorated therein with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their garments therein will be of silk.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: (On the other hand), Allah will cause those who believed and acted righteously to enter the Gardens beneath which rivers flow. They shall be decked in them with bracelets of gold and pearls and their raiment shall be of silk.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Truly, Allah will admit those who believe and do righteous good deeds, to Gardens underneath which rivers flow (in Paradise), wherein they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls and their garments therein will be of silk.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Lo! Allah will cause those who believe and do good works to enter Gardens underneath which rivers flow, wherein they will be allowed armlets of gold, and pearls, and their raiment therein will be silk.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Indeed Allah will admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them, adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearl, and their dress therein will be silk.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: Allah will admit those who believe and do good works to gardens underneath which rivers flow. They shall be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and with pearls, and their garments shall be of silk.

23.author: Saheeh International
