44.text: As well as those of Madyan and Musa (too) was rejected, but I gave respite to the unbelievers, then did I overtake them, so how (severe) was My disapproval.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: and the inhabitants of Midian also charged their prophets with falsehood. Moses was also rejected. I gave respite to those who denied the truth, but then I seized them. Consider then, how terrible My repudiation of them was.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: And the Companions of the Madyan People; and Moses was rejected (in the same way). But I granted respite to the Unbelievers, and (only) after that did I punish them: but how (terrible) was my rejection (of them)!

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Waashabu madyana wakuththiba moosa faamlaytu lilkafireena thumma akhathtuhum fakayfa kana nakeeri

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: How many a habitation given to wickedness have We destroyed, whose roofs tumbled down, which fell into ruins. How many a well and fortress reinforced lie abandoned!

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: And many a township did We destroy, for they were oppressors – so they now lie flat on their roofs, and many a well lying useless and many a palace in ruins.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: How many a city We have destroyed in its evildoing, and now it is fallen down upon its turrets! How many a ruined well, a tall palace!

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: How many a city have We destroyed, while it was a wrong- doer--and it lieth overturned on its roofs, -and how many a well abandoned and how many a castle fortifred!

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: And many a township have We destroyed while it was given to wrong-doing, so that it lies in ruins (up to this day), and (many) a deserted well and lofty castles!

45.author: Itani
45.text: How many a town have We destroyed while it was doing wrong? They lie in ruins; with stilled wells, and lofty mansions.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: How many towns have We destroyed because their people were steeped in iniquity: so they lie fallen down upon their turrets! How many wells lie deserted; and how many towering palaces lie in ruins!

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: And many a township did We destroy while they were given to wrongdoing, so that it lie in ruins, and a deserted well and castle Mashid!

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: How many a township have We destroyed while it was sinful, so that it lieth (to this day) in ruins, and (how many) a deserted well and lofty tower!

45.author: Qarai
45.text: How many towns We have destroyed when they had been wrongdoers! So they lie fallen on their trellises, their wells neglected and their ruined palaces!

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: How many a village We have destroyed in its harmdoing, so that it lies fallen upon its turrets, and how many an abandoned well, and empty palace!

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: And how many a city did We destroy while it was committing wrong - so it is [now] fallen into ruin - and [how many] an abandoned well and [how many] a lofty palace.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: How many were the unjust dwellers of the towns that We destroyed. From their trellises to their lofty mansions, all were toppled and their wells were abandoned.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: So how many a town did We destroy while it was unjust, so it was fallen down upon its roofs, and (how many a) deserted well and palace raised high.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: How many a town We destroyed which was given to wrongdoing, so that its roofs fell down, and how many a well is deserted and how many a lofty castle is in ruins.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: How many populations have We destroyed, which were given to wrong-doing? They tumbled down on their roofs. And how many wells are lying idle and neglected, and castles lofty and well-built?

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Fakaayyin min qaryatin ahlaknaha wahiya thalimatun fahiya khawiyatun AAala AAurooshiha wabirin muAAattalatin waqasrin masheedin

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Have they not travelled in the land that they could have the heart to understand, and ears to hear? It is not the eyes alone that do not see, oblivious are the hearts within their breasts.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: So have they not travelled in the land, to have hearts with which to understand and ears to hear with? So it is not the eyes that are blind, but it is the hearts in the bosoms, that are blind.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: What, have they not journeyed in the land so that they have hearts to understand with or ears to hear with? It is not the eyes that are blind, but blind are the hearts within the breasts.

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: Have they not marched forth in the land, so that there might become unto them hearts to understand with or ears re hear with? Verily it is not the sights that are blinded but blinded are the hearts that are in the breasts.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: Have they not travelled through the land, and have they hearts wherewith to understand and ears wherewith to hear? Verily, it is not the eyes that grow blind, but it is the hearts which are in the breasts that grow blind.

46.author: Itani
46.text: Have they not journeyed in the land, and had minds to reason with, or ears to listen with? It is not the eyes that go blind, but it is the hearts, within the chests, that go blind.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Have they not journeyed in the land that their hearts might understand and their ears might listen? For indeed it is not the eyes that are blinded; it is rather the hearts in the breasts that are rendered blind.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: Have they not traveled through the land, and have they hearts wherewith to understand and ears wherewith to hear Verily, it is not the eyes that grow blind, but it is the hearts which are in the breasts that grow blind.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: Have they not travelled in the land, and have they hearts wherewith to feel and ears wherewith to hear? For indeed it is not the eyes that grow blind, but it is the hearts, which are within the bosoms, that grow blind.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: Have they not travelled through the land so that they may have hearts by which they may exercise their reason, or ears by which they may hear? Indeed, it is not the eyes that turn blind, but it is the hearts in the breasts that turn blind!

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: Have they never journeyed through the land so that they have hearts to understand, or ears to hear with? It is not the eyes, but the hearts in the chests that are blind.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: So have they not traveled through the earth and have hearts by which to reason and ears by which to hear? For indeed, it is not eyes that are blinded, but blinded are the hearts which are within the breasts.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: Have they not travelled (sufficiently) in the land to have understanding hearts and listening ears? It is their hearts which are blind, not their ears.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: Have they not travelled in the land so that they should have hearts with which to understand, or ears with which to hear? For surely it is not the eyes that are blind, but blind are the hearts which are in the breasts.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: Have these people not travelled through the land to make their hearts understand and let their ears hear; the truth is that it is not the eyes that are blind but the hearts that are in the bosoms that are blinded.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: Do they not travel through the land, so that their hearts (and minds) may thus learn wisdom and their ears may thus learn to hear? Truly it is not their eyes that are blind, but their hearts which are in their breasts.

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Afalam yaseeroo fee alardi fatakoona lahum quloobun yaAAqiloona biha aw athanun yasmaAAoona biha fainnaha la taAAma alabsaru walakin taAAma alquloobu allatee fee alssudoori

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: That is how they ask you to hasten the punishment; but God does not go back on His promise. Verily a day with your Lord is equal by your reckoning to a thousand years.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And they ask you for the punishment – they are impatient – and Allah will not make His promise untrue; and indeed a single day before Allah is like a thousand years in your calculation.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: And they demand of thee to hasten the chastisement! God will not fail His promise; and surely a day with thy Lord is as a thousand years of your counting.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And they ask thee to hasten on the torment, whereas Allah shall not fail His promise. And verily a day with thy Lord is as a thousand years of that which ye compute.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And they ask you to hasten on the torment! And Allah fails not His Promise. And verily, a day with your Lord is as a thousand years of what you reckon.

47.author: Itani
47.text: And they ask you to hasten the punishment. But God never breaks His promise. A day with your Lord is like a thousand years of your count.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment. Allah shall most certainly not fail His promise; but a Day with your Lord is as a thousand years of your reckoning.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And they ask you to hasten on the torment! And Allah fails not His promise. And verily a day with your Lord is as a thousand years of what you reckon.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: And they will bid thee hasten on the Doom, and Allah faileth not His promise, but lo! a Day with Allah is as a thousand years of what ye reckon.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment, though Allah will never break His promise. Indeed a day with your Lord is like a thousand years by your reckoning.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment. Allah will not break His promise. Each day with your Lord is like a thousand years in your reckoning.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And they urge you to hasten the punishment. But Allah will never fail in His promise. And indeed, a day with your Lord is like a thousand years of those which you count.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: They want you to bring upon them their punishment without delay. God never disregards His promise. One day for God is equal to a thousand years for you.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And they ask you to hasten on the punishment, and Allah will by no means fail in His promise, and surely a day with your Lord is as a thousand years of what you number.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: They ask you to hasten the punishment; God will never go back on His promise. A Day with your Lord is like a thousand years in your reckoning.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Yet they ask thee to hasten on the Punishment! But Allah will not fail in His Promise. Verily a Day in the sight of thy Lord is like a thousand years of your reckoning.

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: WayastaAAjiloonaka bialAAathabi walan yukhlifa Allahu waAAdahu wainna yawman AAinda rabbika kaalfi sanatin mimma taAAuddoona

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: To how many habitations did We give respite, though given to wickedness, and then seized them. To Me they had to come back in the end.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And to many a township did I give respite although they were oppressors; then I seized them; and towards Me is the return.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: How many a city I have respited in its evildoing; then I seized it, and to Me was the homecoming.

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And how many a city did give reins to, While it wronged itself, then took hold of it! and unto Me is the return.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: And many a township did I give respite while it was given to wrong-doing. Then (in the end) I seized it (with punishment). And to Me is the (final) return (of all).

48.author: Itani
48.text: How many a town have I reprieved, although it was unjust? Then I seized it. To Me is the destination.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: How many towns did I respite at first though they were steeped in iniquity, and then I seized them! To Me are all destined to return.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And many a township did I give respite while it was given to wrongdoing. Then I seized it (with punishment). And to Me is the (final) return (of all).

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: And how many a township did I suffer long though it was sinful! Then I grasped it. Unto Me is the return.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: To how many a town did I give respite while it was doing wrong! Then I seized it, and toward Me is the destination.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: And how many a village have I respited in its evil doing! Then I seized it. To Me is the return.

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And for how many a city did I prolong enjoyment while it was committing wrong. Then I seized it, and to Me is the [final] destination.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: To how many unjust towns have We given respite and then sized with torment. To Me do all things return.

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And how many a town to which I gave respite while it was unjust, then I overtook it, and to Me is the return.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: To how many a town We gave respite while it was given to wrongdoing. Then I seized it. To Me all things shall return.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: And to how many populations did I give respite, which were given to wrong-doing? in the end I punished them. To me is the destination (of all).

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Wakaayyin min qaryatin amlaytu laha wahiya thalimatun thumma akhathtuha wailayya almaseeru

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Tell them: "O men, it's my duty to warn you clearly."

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Proclaim, “O mankind, I am for you only a clear Herald of Warning.”

49.author: Arberry
49.text: Say: 'O men, I am only for you a plain warner.'

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Say thou: mankind! I am unto you only a warner manifest.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "O mankind! I am (sent) to you only as a plain warner."

49.author: Itani
49.text: Say, “O people, I am only a plain warner to you.”

49.author: Maududi
49.text: Say (O Muhammad): "O people! I have been sent to you only as a plain warner (before the Doom strikes you)."

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Say: "O mankind! I am (sent) to you only as a plain warner."

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Say: O mankind! I am only a plain warner unto you.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Say, ‘O mankind! I am only a manifest warner to you!’

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: Say (Prophet Muhammad): 'O people, I have been sent to warn you plainly.

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Say, "O people, I am only to you a clear warner."

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "People, I am giving you a clear warning.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Say: O people! I am only a plain warner to you.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: Say, "O people, I am sent only to give you clear warning."

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Say: "O men! I am (sent) to you only to give a Clear Warning:

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Qul ya ayyuha alnnasu innama ana lakum natheerun mubeenun

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: For those who believe and do the right is forgiveness and gracious provision.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: So those who believed and did good deeds, for them are forgiveness, and an excellent sustenance.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: Those who believe, and do deeds of righteousness -- theirs shall be forgiveness and generous provision.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: Then those who believe and work righteous works--for them is forgiveness and a provision honourable.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: So those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, for them is forgiveness and Rizqun Karim (generous provision, i.e. Paradise).

50.author: Itani
50.text: Those who believe and work righteousness—for them is forgiveness and a generous provision.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: So those who believe and act righteously shall be granted forgiveness and an honourable sustenance,

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: So those who believe and do righteous good deeds, for them is forgiveness and Rizq Karim.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: Those who believe and do good works, for them is pardon and a rich provision;

50.author: Qarai
50.text: As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, for them will be forgiveness and a noble provision.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: Those who believe and do good works, theirs shall be forgiveness and a generous provision;

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And those who have believed and done righteous deeds - for them is forgiveness and noble provision.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: The righteously striving believers will receive forgiveness and honorable sustenance.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: Then (as for) those who believe and do good, they shall have forgiveness and an honorable sustenance.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: Those who believe and do good deeds shall be forgiven and shall receive an honourable provision.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: "Those who believe and work righteousness, for them is forgiveness and a sustenance most generous.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Faallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati lahum maghfiratun warizqun kareemun

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: But those who try to defeat Our signs are people of Hell.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And those who strive in Our signs with the intention of disputing, are the people of fire.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: And those who strive against Our signs to void them -- they shall be the inhabitants of Hell.

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And those who endeavour in respect of Our signs to frustrate them- those shall be the fellows of Flaming Fire.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: But those who strive against Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), to frustrate and obstruct them, they will be dwellers of the Hell-fire.

51.author: Itani
51.text: But those who strive against Our revelations—these are the inmates of Hell.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: whereas those who strive against Our Signs, seeking to profane them, they are the friends of the Fire!

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: But those who strive against Our Ayat to frustrate them, they will be dwellers of the Hellfire.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: While those who strive to thwart Our revelations, such are rightful owners of the Fire.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: But as for those who contend with Our signs, seeking to frustrate [their purpose], they shall be the inmates of hell.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: but those who strive to void Our verses, thinking they have escaped, shall be the people of Hell'

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: But the ones who strove against Our verses, [seeking] to cause failure - those are the companions of Hellfire.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: Those who try to challenge Our miracles will be the dwellers of hell."

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And (as for) those who strive to oppose Our communications, they shall be the inmates of the flaming fire.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: Whereas those who strive against Our signs, seeking to defeat their purpose, shall be the inmates of the Fire.

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: "But those who strive against Our Signs, to frustrate them,- they will be Companions of the Fire."

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Waallatheena saAAaw fee ayatina muAAajizeena olaika ashabu aljaheemi

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: We have sent no messenger or apostle before you with whose recitations Satan did not tamper. Yet God abrogates what Satan interpolates; then He confirms His revelations, for God is all-knowing and all-wise.

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: And all the Noble Messengers or Prophets whom We sent before you – it occurred with all of them – that whenever they recited (the message) Satan included a bit (from his own speech) in their recitation to the people; so Allah obliterates what Satan includes and then Allah fortifies His verses; and Allah is All Knowing, Wise.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: We sent not ever any Messenger or Prophet before thee, but that Satan cast into his fancy, when he was fancying; but God annuls what Satan casts, then God confirms His signs -- surely God is All-knowing, All-wise --

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: And We have sent before thee no apostle or prophet but when he read the Satan cast forth suggestions in respect of his reading; then Allah abolisheth that which the Satan casteth forth; then Allah confirmeth His revelations; and Allah is Knowing. Wise;

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: Never did We send a Messenger or a Prophet before you, but; when he did recite the revelation or narrated or spoke, Shaitan (Satan) threw (some falsehood) in it. But Allah abolishes that which Shaitan (Satan) throws in. Then Allah establishes His Revelations. And Allah is All-Knower, All-Wise:

52.author: Itani
52.text: We never sent a messenger before you, or a prophet, but when he had a desire Satan interfered in his wishes. But God nullifies what Satan interjects, and God affirms His revelations. God is Omniscient and Wise.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: Never did We send a Messenger or a Prophet before you (O Muhammad), but that whenever he had a desire, Satan interfered with that desire. Allah eradicates the interference of Satan and strengthens His Signs. Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: Never did We send a Messenger or a Prophet before you but when he did recite (the revelation or narrated or spoke), Shaytan threw (some falsehood) in his recitation (of the revelation). But Yansakh Allah that which Shaytan throws in. Then Allah establishes His revelations. And Allah is All-Knower, All-Wise:

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: Never sent We a messenger or a prophet before thee but when He recited (the message) Satan proposed (opposition) in respect of that which he recited thereof. But Allah abolisheth that which Satan proposeth. Then Allah establisheth His revelations. Allah is Knower, Wise;

52.author: Qarai
52.text: We did not send any apostle or prophet before you but that when he recited [the scripture] Satan interjected [something] in his recitation. Thereat Allah nullifies whatever Satan has interjected, and then Allah confirms His signs, and Allah is All-knowing, All-wise.

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: Never have We sent a Messenger or a Prophet before you, but when he hoped, satan tampered with his hope. But Allah supersedes the tampering of satan and confirms His verses. And Allah is the Knower, the Wise.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: And We did not send before you any messenger or prophet except that when he spoke [or recited], Satan threw into it [some misunderstanding]. But Allah abolishes that which Satan throws in; then Allah makes precise His verses. And Allah is Knowing and Wise.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: Satan would try to tamper with the desires of every Prophet or Messenger whom We sent. Then God would remove Satan's temptations and strengthen His revelations. God is All-knowing and All-wise.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: And We did not send before you any apostle or prophet, but when he desired, the Shaitan made a suggestion respecting his desire; but Allah annuls that which the Shaitan casts, then does Allah establish His communications, and Allah is Knowing, Wise,

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: Whenever We sent any messenger or prophet before you, and he recited anything [of Our revelation], Satan tampered with it. But God abrogates Satan's interjections and then He firmly reaffirms His revelations. God is all knowing and all wise.

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: Never did We send a messenger or a prophet before thee, but, when he framed a desire, Satan threw some (vanity) into his desire: but Allah will cancel anything (vain) that Satan throws in, and Allah will confirm (and establish) His Signs: for Allah is full of Knowledge and Wisdom:

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Wama arsalna min qablika min rasoolin wala nabiyyin illa itha tamanna alqa alshshaytanu fee omniyyatihi fayansakhu Allahu ma yulqee alshshaytanu thumma yuhkimu Allahu ayatihi waAllahu AAaleemun hakeemun

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: This is in order to make the interpolations of Satan a test for those whose hearts are diseased and hardened: Surely the sinners have gone far in dissent.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: So that He may make what the devil includes a trial for those in whose hearts is a disease, and those whose hearts are hardened; indeed the unjust are extremely quarrelsome.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: that He may make what Satan casts a trial for those in whose hearts is sickness, and those whose hearts are hard; and surely the evildoers are in wide schism;

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: That he may make that which the Satan casteth forth a temptation for those in whose heart is a disease and whose hearts are hardened--and the wrong-doers are in divergence far-off -

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: That He (Allah) may make what is thrown in by Shaitan (Satan) a trial for those in whose hearts is a disease (of hypocrisy and disbelief) and whose hearts are hardened. And certainly, the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.) are in an opposition far-off (from the truth against Allah's Messenger and the believers).

53.author: Itani
53.text: In order to make Satan’s suggestions a trial for those whose hearts are diseased, and those whose hearts are hardened. The wrongdoers are in profound discord.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: (He does this) in order that He may make the evil caused by Satan a trial for those in whose hearts there is sickness (of hypocrisy), whose hearts are hard (and vitiated). Surely these wrong-doers have gone too far in their dissension.

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: That He (Allah) may make what is thrown in by Shaytan a trial for those in whose hearts is a disease and whose hearts are hardened. And certainly, the wrongdoers are in an opposition far-off (from the truth).

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: That He may make that which the devil proposeth a temptation for those in whose hearts is a disease, and those whose hearts are hardened - Lo! the evil-doers are in open schism -

53.author: Qarai
53.text: That He may make what Satan has thrown in a test for those in whose hearts is a sickness and those whose hearts have hardened. Indeed the wrongdoers are steeped in extreme defiance.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: (This He permits) in order that He makes satan's interjections a temptation for those in whose hearts is a sickness and those whose hearts are hardened and the harmdoers are in a wide schism,

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: [That is] so He may make what Satan throws in a trial for those within whose hearts is disease and those hard of heart. And indeed, the wrongdoers are in extreme dissension.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: He would make Satan's temptations a trial for those whose hearts are hard and sick. The wrong-doers are far away from the Lord,

53.author: Shakir
53.text: So that He may make what the Shaitan casts a trial for those in whose hearts is disease and those whose hearts are hard; and most surely the unjust are in a great opposition,

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: He makes Satan's suggestions a trial for those whose hearts are diseased or hardened, and, surely, the wrongdoers are far gone in error,

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: That He may make the suggestions thrown in by Satan, but a trial for those in whose hearts is a disease and who are hardened of heart: verily the wrong-doers are in a schism far (from the Truth):

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: LiyajAAala ma yulqee alshshaytanu fitnatan lillatheena fee quloobihim maradun waalqasiyati quloobuhum wainna alththalimeena lafee shiqaqin baAAeedin

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: At the same time those who have been given knowledge may know that this is the truth from your Lord, and come to believe in it, and their hearts become submissive to Him. Verily God guides those who believe, to the even path.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: And so that the people given the knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord, in order that they may accept faith in it, therefore their hearts may humble before Him; and indeed Allah will guide the believers on the Straight Path.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: and that they who have been given knowledge may know that it is the truth from thy Lord and believe in it, and so their hearts be humble unto Him; and assuredly God ever guides those who believe to a straight path.

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: And that those who have been vouchsafed knowledge may know that it is the truth from thy Lord and may believe therein, and so their hearts may submit thereto. And verily Allah is the Guide of those who believe unto a path straight.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: And that those who have been given knowledge may know that it (this Quran) is the truth from your Lord, and that they may believe therein, and their hearts may submit to it with humility. And verily, Allah is the Guide of those who believe, to the Straight Path.

54.author: Itani
54.text: And so that those endowed with knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord, and so believe in it, and their hearts soften to it. God guides those who believe to a straight path.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: (He also does this) in order that those endowed with knowledge may know that it is the Truth from your Lord and that they may have faith in it and their hearts may humble themselves before Him. Verily Allah always directs those that believe to the Right Way.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: And that those who have been given knowledge may know that it (this Qur'an) is the truth from your Lord, so that they may believe therein, and their hearts may submit to it with humility. And verily, Allah is the Guide of those who believe, to the straight path.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: And that those who have been given knowledge may know that it is the truth from thy Lord, so that they may believe therein and their hearts may submit humbly unto Him. Lo! Allah verily is guiding those who believe unto a right path.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: [And also for the reason] that those who have been given knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord, and so they may have faith in it, and their hearts may be humbled before Him. Indeed Allah guides those who have faith to a straight path.

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: and so that those to whom knowledge has been given will know that this is the truth from your Lord and so believe in it and that their hearts will be humble to Him. Indeed, Allah will surely guide those who believe to a Straight Path.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: And so those who were given knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord and [therefore] believe in it, and their hearts humbly submit to it. And indeed is Allah the Guide of those who have believed to a straight path.

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: so that those who have received knowledge would know and believe that whatever happens with the Prophets and Messengers is the Truth from their Lord and will believe it. This will cause their hearts to become filled with awe. God guides the believers to the right path.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: And that those who have been given the knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord, so they may believe in it and their hearts may be lowly before it; and most surely Allah is the Guide of those who believe into a right path.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: so that those who are given knowledge may realize that this is the truth from your Lord and thus believe in it, and so that in their hearts they may humbly submit to Him. God will surely guide the faithful to a straight path.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: And that those on whom knowledge has been bestowed may learn that the (Qur'an) is the Truth from thy Lord, and that they may believe therein, and their hearts may be made humbly (open) to it: for verily Allah is the Guide of those who believe, to the Straight Way.

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: WaliyaAAlama allatheena ootoo alAAilma annahu alhaqqu min rabbika fayuminoo bihi fatukhbita lahu quloobuhum wainna Allaha lahadi allatheena amanoo ila siratin mustaqeemin

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: The infidels will remain in doubt about it till the Hour overtakes them unawares, or the punishment of the barren day destructive should come upon them.

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: And the disbelievers will always be in doubt of it, to the extent that the Last Day will suddenly come upon them, or will come upon them the punishment of a day the result of which is not at all good for them.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: And the unbelievers will not cease to be in doubt of it, until the Hour comes on them suddenly, or there shall come upon them the chastisement of a barren day.

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: And those who disbelieve will not cease to be in doubt thereof until the Hour cometh upon them on a sudden, or there cometh upon them the torment of a Barren Day.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: And those who disbelieve will not cease to be in doubt about it (this Quran) until the Hour comes suddenly upon them, or there comes to them the torment of the Day after which there will be no night (i.e. the Day of Resurrection).

55.author: Itani
55.text: Those who disbelieve will continue to be hesitant about it, until the Hour comes upon them suddenly, or there comes to them the torment of a desolate Day.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: The unbelievers will not cease to be in doubt about it until the Hour suddenly comes upon them, or the chastisement of an ominous day overtakes them.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: And those who disbelieved, will not cease to be in doubt about it until the Hour comes suddenly upon them, or there comes to them the torment of Yawm `Aqim.

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: And those who disbelieve will not cease to be in doubt thereof until the Hour come upon them unawares, or there come unto them the doom of a disastrous day.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: Those who are faithless persist in their doubt about it, until either the Hour overtakes them suddenly, or they are overtaken by the punishment of an inauspicious day.

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: The unbelievers will never cease to doubt it until the Hour overtakes them suddenly or the punishment of the Barren Day comes upon them.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: But those who disbelieve will not cease to be in doubt of it until the Hour comes upon them unexpectedly or there comes to them the punishment of a barren Day.

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: The unbelievers will continue to doubt the Quran until the Hour of Doom suddenly sizes them or the torment of the last day strikes them.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: And those who disbelieve shall not cease to be in doubt concerning it until the hour overtakes them suddenly, or there comes on them the chastisement of a destructive day.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: Those who deny the truth will continue in doubt until the [Last] Hour suddenly comes upon them or the scourge of the woeful Day descends upon them.

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: Those who reject Faith will not cease to be in doubt concerning (Revelation) until the Hour (of Judgment) comes suddenly upon them, or there comes to them the Penalty of a Day of Disaster.

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Wala yazalu allatheena kafaroo fee miryatin minhu hatta tatiyahumu alssaAAatu baghtatan aw yatiyahum AAathabu yawmin AAaqeemin

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: The order will be God's on that Day; He will judge between them. Then those who had come to believe and done the right will be in gardens of delight.

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: For Allah only is the kingship on that day; He will judge between them; so those who believed and did good deeds are in Gardens of content.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: The Kingdom upon that day shall belong to God, and He shall judge between them. As for those who believe, and do deeds of righteousness, they shall be in Gardens of Bliss.

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: The dominion on that Day will be Allah's; He shall judge between them; then those who believed and worked righteous works shall be in the Gardens of Delight.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: The sovereignty on that Day will be that of Allah (the one Who has no partners). He will judge between them. So those who believed (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and did righteous good deeds will be in Gardens of delight (Paradise).

56.author: Itani
56.text: Sovereignty on that Day belongs to God; He will judge between them. Those who believe and do good deeds will be in the Gardens of Bliss.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: On that Day all sovereignty shall be Allah's and He will judge among them. Then those who believed and acted righteously shall be in Gardens of Bliss.

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: The sovereignty on that Day will be that of Allah. He will judge between them. So those who believed and did righteous good deeds will be in Gardens of Delight.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: The Sovereignty on that day will be Allah's, He will judge between them. Then those who believed and did good works will be in Gardens of Delight,

56.author: Qarai
56.text: On that day all sovereignty will belong to Allah: He will judge between them; then those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be in gardens of bliss,

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: The Kingdom upon that Day shall belong to Allah. He will judge between them. Those who believe and do good works shall be in Gardens of Bliss,

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: [All] sovereignty that Day is for Allah; He will judge between them. So they who believed and did righteous deeds will be in the Gardens of Pleasure.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: On that day it is God who will be the Absolute King and Judge of (mankind). The righteously striving believers will go to Paradise

56.author: Shakir
56.text: The kingdom on that day shall be Allah's; He will judge between them; so those who believe and do good will be in gardens of bliss.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: On that Day all control will belong to God. He will judge between them. Those who believe and do good deeds shall enter the Gardens of Bliss,

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: On that Day of Dominion will be that of Allah: He will judge between them: so those who believe and work righteous deeds will be in Gardens of Delight.

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Almulku yawmaithin lillahi yahkumu baynahum faallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati fee jannati alnnaAAeemi

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: But those who did not believe and called Our revelations lies, will be given disgraceful punishment.

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: And those who disbelieved and denied Our signs – for them will be a disgraceful punishment.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: But as for the unbelievers, who cried lies to Our signs; for them awaits a humbling chastisement.

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: And those who disbelieved and belied Our signs--then these! for them will be a torment ignominous.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: And those who disbelieved and belied Our Verses (of this Quran), for them will be a humiliating torment (in Hell).

57.author: Itani
57.text: But those who disbelieve and reject Our revelations—these will have a humiliating punishment.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: A humiliating chastisement awaits those who disbelieved and denied Our Signs.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: And those who disbelieved and denied Our Ayat, for them will be a humiliating torment (in Hell).

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: While those who disbelieved and denied Our revelations, for them will be a shameful doom.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: and those who are faithless and deny Our signs—for such there will be a humiliating punishment.

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: but for those who disbelieved Our verses and belied there awaits a humbling punishment.

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: And they who disbelieved and denied Our signs - for those there will be a humiliating punishment.

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: and the unbelievers who called Our revelations lies will suffer humiliating torment.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: And (as for) those who disbelieve in and reject Our communications, these it is who shall have a disgraceful chastisement.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: but those who deny the truth and deny Our signs will receive a humiliating punishment.

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: And for those who reject Faith and deny our Signs, there will be a humiliating Punishment.

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: Waallatheena kafaroo wakaththaboo biayatina faolaika lahum AAathabun muheenun

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: Those who left their homes in the way of God, and then were killed or died, will surely be given a better provision by God, for God is surely the best of providers.

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And those who left their homes and belongings in Allah's cause and were then killed or died – Allah will therefore indeed provide for them an excellent sustenance; and indeed the sustenance bestowed by Allah is the best.

58.author: Arberry
58.text: And those who emigrated in God's way and were slain, or died, God shall provide them with a fair provision; and surely God is the best of providers.

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And those who emigrated in the way of Allah, and thereafter they were slain or they died - surely Allah will provide them with a goodly provision; and verily Allah! He is the Best Provider.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: Those who emigrated in the Cause of Allah and after that were killed or died, surely, Allah will provide a good provision for them. And verily, it is Allah Who indeed is the Best of those who make provision.

58.author: Itani
58.text: Those who emigrate in God’s cause, then get killed, or die, God will provide them with fine provisions. God is the Best of Providers.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: As for those who migrated in the way of Allah, whereafter they were slain, or died, Allah will certainly grant them a goodly provision. Indeed, Allah is the Best of all those who provide.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: Those who emigrated in the cause of Allah and after that were killed or died, surely, Allah will provide a good provision for them. And verily, it is Allah Who indeed is the Best of those who make provision.

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: Those who fled their homes for the cause of Allah and then were slain or died, Allah verily will provide for them a good provision. Lo! Allah, He verily is Best of all who make provision.

58.author: Qarai
58.text: Those who migrate in the way of Allah and then are slain, or die, Allah will surely provide them with a good provision. Allah is indeed the best of providers.

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: As for those who emigrated in the way of Allah and were slain, or died, Allah will provide them with fine provisions. Allah is the Best of providers.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And those who emigrated for the cause of Allah and then were killed or died - Allah will surely provide for them a good provision. And indeed, it is Allah who is the best of providers.

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: Those who abandoned their homes for the cause of God and who then died or were murdered will receive honorable sustenance from God; He is the Most Generous and Munificent.

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And (as for) those who fly in Allah's way and are then slain or die, Allah will most certainly grant them a goodly sustenance, and most surely Allah is the best Giver of sustenance.

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: As for those who left their homes for the cause of God and then were slain or died, God will give them a generous provision. Surely God is the Best of Providers.

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: Those who leave their homes in the cause of Allah, and are then slain or die,- On them will Allah bestow verily a goodly Provision: Truly Allah is He Who bestows the best provision.

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Waallatheena hajaroo fee sabeeli Allahi thumma qutiloo aw matoo layarzuqannahumu Allahu rizqan hasanan wainna Allaha lahuwa khayru alrraziqeena

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: God will surely lead them to a place with which they will be gratified. Verily God is all-knowing and forbearing.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: He will certainly admit them to a place they will love; and indeed Allah is All Knowing, Most Forbearing.

59.author: Arberry
59.text: He shall admit them by a gate that is well-pleasing to them; and surely God is All-knowing, All-clement.

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Surely He will make them enter an entrance wherewith they will be well pleased and verily Allah is Knowing, Forbearing.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Truly, He will make them enter an entrance with which they shall be well-pleased, and verily, Allah indeed is All-Knowing, Most Forbearing.

59.author: Itani
59.text: He will admit them an admittance that will please them. God is Knowing and Clement.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: He will surely admit them to a resort which will please them. Most certainly Allah is All-Knowing, Most Forbearing.

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Truly, He will make them enter an entrance with which they shall be well-pleased, and verily, Allah indeed is All-Knowing, Most Forbearing.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Assuredly He will cause them to enter by an entry that they will love. Lo! Allah verily is Knower, Indulgent.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: He will admit them into an abode they are pleased with. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-forbearing.

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: He will admit them by a gate that is pleasing to them, and surely, Allah is the Knower, the Clement.

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: He will surely cause them to enter an entrance with which they will be pleased, and indeed, Allah is Knowing and Forbearing.

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: God will certainly admit them to a pleasant dwelling. God is All-knowing and Forbearing.

59.author: Shakir
59.text: He will certainly cause them to enter a place of entrance which they shall be well pleased with, and most surely Allah is Knowing, Forbearing.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: He will admit them to a place with which they shall be well-pleased. For God is all knowing and most forbearing.

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: Verily He will admit them to a place with which they shall be well pleased: for Allah is All-Knowing, Most Forbearing.

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Layudkhilannahum mudkhalan yardawnahu wainna Allaha laAAaleemun haleemun

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: Whosoever retaliates to the extent of injury suffered by him, and is wronged again, will certainly be helped by God. Verily God is forgiving and kind.

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: So it is; and whoever retaliates similarly to the affliction he was made to suffer, and then he is wronged again – so Allah will definitely assist him; indeed Allah is Oft Pardoning, Oft Forgiving.

60.author: Arberry
60.text: All that; and whosoever chastises after the manner that he was chastised and then again is oppressed, assuredly God will help him; surely God is All-pardoning, All-forgiving.

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: That is so. And whosoever Chastiseth the like of that whereby He was injured, and thereafter he hath been ogain oppressed, surely Allah will succour him: verily Allah is Pardoning, Forgiving.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: That is so. And whoever has retaliated with the like of that which he was made to suffer, and then has again been wronged, Allah will surely help him. Verily! Allah indeed is Oft-Pardoning, Oft-Forgiving.

60.author: Itani
60.text: That is so! Whoever retaliates similarly to the affliction he was made to suffer, and then he is wronged again, God will definitely assist him. God is Pardoning and Forgiving.

60.author: Maududi
60.text: That indeed is so, as for him who retaliates in proportion to the excess committed against him, and is thereafter again subjected to transgression, Allah will surely aid him. Verily Allah is All-Pardoning, All-Forgiving.

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: That is so. And whoever has retaliated with the like of that which he was made to suffer, and then has again been wronged, Allah will surely help him. Verily, Allah indeed is Oft-Pardoning, Oft-Forgiving.

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: That (is so). And whoso hath retaliated with the like of that which he was made to suffer and then hath (again) been wronged, Allah will succour him. Lo! Allah verily is Mild, Forgiving.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: So will it be; and whoever retaliates with the like of what he has been made to suffer, and is aggressed against [again] thereafter, Allah will surely help him. Indeed Allah is all-excusing, all-forgiving.

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: So shall it be. He who punishes after the manner that he was punished and then again is oppressed, shall be helped by Allah. Allah is Pardoning, Forgiving.

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: That [is so]. And whoever responds [to injustice] with the equivalent of that with which he was harmed and then is tyrannized - Allah will surely aid him. Indeed, Allah is Pardoning and Forgiving.

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: One who is wronged and who retaliates by that which is equal to his suffering, God will certainly help him; He is All-pardoning and All-forgiving.

60.author: Shakir
60.text: That (shall be so); and he who retaliates with the like of that with which he has been afflicted and he has been oppressed, Allah will most certainly aid him; most surely Allah is Pardoning, Forgiving.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: Thus it shall be. As for one who retaliates to the same extent as he has suffered and then is again wronged, God will surely come to his aid. God is merciful and forgiving.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: That (is so). And if one has retaliated to no greater extent than the injury he received, and is again set upon inordinately, Allah will help him: for Allah is One that blots out (sins) and forgives (again and again).

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Thalika waman AAaqaba bimithli ma AAooqiba bihi thumma bughiya AAalayhi layansurannahu Allahu inna Allaha laAAafuwwun ghafoorun

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: That is so for God turns night into day and day into night, for God is all-hearing and all-seeing;

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: This is because Allah inserts the night into a part of the day and inserts the day into a part of the night, and because Allah is All Hearing, All Seeing.

61.author: Arberry
61.text: That is because God makes the night to enter into the day and makes the day to enter into the night; and that God is All-hearing, All-seeing.

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: That is to be because Allah plungeth the night into the day and plungeth the day into the night, and because He is Hearing, Beholding.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: That is because Allah merges the night into the day, and He merges the day into the night. And verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Seer.

61.author: Itani
61.text: That is because God merges the night into the day, and He merges the day into the night, and because God is Hearing and Seeing.

61.author: Maududi
61.text: So shall it be because it is Allah Who causes the night to emerge out of the day and causes the day to emerge out of the night and Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: That is because Allah merges the night into the day, and He merges the day into the night. And verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Seer.

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: That is because Allah maketh the night to pass into the day and maketh the day to pass into the night, and because Allah is Hearer, Seer.

61.author: Qarai
61.text: So will it be, because Allah makes the night pass into the day and makes the day pass into the night, and because Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing.

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: That is because Allah causes the night to enter into the day, and the day enter into the night. Allah is the Hearer and the Seer.

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: That is because Allah causes the night to enter the day and causes the day to enter the night and because Allah is Hearing and Seeing.

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: God causes the night to enter the day and the day to enter the night. He is All-hearing and All-aware.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: That is because Allah causes the night to enter into the day and causes the day to enter into the night, and because Allah is Hearing, Seeing.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: That is because God makes the night pass into the day and makes the day pass into the night. God is all hearing and all seeing.

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: That is because Allah merges night into day, and He merges day into night, and verily it is Allah Who hears and sees (all things).

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Thalika bianna Allaha yooliju allayla fee alnnahari wayooliju alnnahara fee allayli waanna Allaha sameeAAun baseerun

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: That is so for God is the undeniable truth, and what they invoke apart from Him is false; yet God is all-high and supreme.

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: This is because Allah only is the Truth, and what they worship other than Him, is itself falsehood, and because Allah only is the Supreme, the Great.

62.author: Arberry
62.text: That is because God -- He is the Truth, and that they call upon apart from Him -- that is the false; and for that God is the All-high, the All-great.

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: That is because Allah! He is the Truth, and because that which they call upon beside Him--it is the false. And verily Allah! He is the High, the Great.

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: That is because Allah He is the Truth (the only True God of all that exists, Who has no partners or rivals with Him), and what they (the polytheists) invoke besides Him, it is Batil (falsehood) And verily, Allah He is the Most High, the Most Great.

62.author: Itani
62.text: That is because God is the Reality, and what they invoke besides Him is vanity, and because God is the Sublime, the Grand.

62.author: Maududi
62.text: So shall it be because Allah, He is the Truth, and all whom they invoke instead of Him are false. Allah is Most High, All-Great.

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: That is because Allah -- He is the Truth, and what they invoke besides Him, it is false. And verily, Allah -- He is the Most High, the Most Great.

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: That is because Allah, He is the True, and that whereon they call instead of Him, it is the false, and because Allah, He is the High, the Great.

62.author: Qarai
62.text: So will it be, because Allah is the Reality, and what they invoke besides Him is nullity, and because Allah is the All-exalted, the All-great.

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: That is because Allah is the Truth, and falsehood is all that they call upon, other than Him. Allah is the Most High, the Most Great.

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: That is because Allah is the Truth, and that which they call upon other than Him is falsehood, and because Allah is the Most High, the Grand.

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: God is the Supreme Truth and whatever they worship instead of Him is falsehood. God is most Exalted and most Great.

62.author: Shakir
62.text: That is because Allah is the Truth, and that what they call upon besides Him-- that is the falsehood, and because Allah is the High, the Great.

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: That is because God is the Truth while anything they invoke besides God is sheer falsehood. God is the Sublime, the Great One.

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: That is because Allah - He is the Reality; and those besides Him whom they invoke,- they are but vain Falsehood: verily Allah is He, Most High, Most Great.

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Thalika bianna Allaha huwa alhaqqu waanna ma yadAAoona min doonihi huwa albatilu waanna Allaha huwa alAAaliyyu alkabeeru

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: Do you not see how God sends down water from the sky and in the morning the earth turns green? Truly God is benign and well-informed.

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: Did you not see that Allah sent down water from the sky, so the earth became green at morn? Indeed Allah is Pure, Aware.

63.author: Arberry
63.text: Hast thou not seen how that God has sent down out of heaven water, and in the morning the earth becomes green? God is All-subtle, All-aware.

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: Beholdest thou not that Allah sendeth down water from the heaven and the earth becometh green? Verily Allah is subtile, Aware.

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: See you not that Allah sends down water (rain) from the sky, and then the earth becomes green? Verily, Allah is the Most Kind and Courteous, Well-Acquainted with all things.

63.author: Itani
63.text: Do you not see that God sends down water from the sky, and the land becomes green? God is Kind and Aware.

63.author: Maududi
63.text: Do you not see that Allah sends down water from the sky whereby the earth turns green? Verily Allah is Subtle, All-Aware.

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: See you not that Allah sends down water from the sky, and then the earth becomes green Verily, Allah is the Most Kind and Courteous, Well-Acquainted with all things.

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Seest thou not how Allah sendeth down water from the sky and then the earth becometh green upon the morrow? Lo! Allah is Subtile, Aware.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: Have you not regarded that Allah sends down water from the sky, whereupon the earth turns green? Indeed Allah is all-attentive, all-aware.

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: Do you not see that Allah sends down water from the sky and in the morning the earth becomes green? Allah is Subtle, the Aware.

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: Do you not see that Allah has sent down rain from the sky and the earth becomes green? Indeed, Allah is Subtle and Acquainted.

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: Have you not seen that God has sent water from the sky and has made the earth green all over. He is Kind and All-aware.

63.author: Shakir
63.text: Do you not see that Allah sends down water from the cloud so the earth becomes green? Surely Allah is Benignant, Aware.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: Have you not seen how God sends down water from sky, whereupon the earth becomes green? God is unfathomable, and all aware;

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: Seest thou not that Allah sends down rain from the sky, and forthwith the earth becomes clothed with green? for Allah is He Who understands the finest mysteries, and is well-acquainted (with them).

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Alam tara anna Allaha anzala mina alssamai maan fatusbihu alardu mukhdarratan inna Allaha lateefun khabeerun

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: Whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him. Surely God is all-sufficing, worthy of praise.

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: To Him only belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth; and indeed Allah only is the Perfect (Not Needing Anything), the Most Praiseworthy.

64.author: Arberry
64.text: To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and in the earth; surely God -- He is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable.

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: His is whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is on the earth; and verily Allah He is the Self-sufficient, the PraiseWorthy.

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth. And verily, Allah He is Rich (Free of all wants), Worthy of all praise.

64.author: Itani
64.text: To Him belongs everything in the heavens and everything on earth. God is the Rich, the Praised.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth. Surely Allah - He alone is Self- Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth. And verily, Allah -- He is Rich, Worthy of all praise.

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: Unto Him belongeth all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth. Lo! Allah, He verily is the Absolute, the Owner of Praise.

64.author: Qarai
64.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, Indeed Allah is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable.

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: To Him belong all that is in the heavens and earth, surely, Allah is the Rich, the Praised.

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: To Him belongs what is in the heavens and what is on the earth. And indeed, Allah is the Free of need, the Praiseworthy.

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth. God is Self-sufficient and Praiseworthy.

64.author: Shakir
64.text: His is whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth; and most surely Allah is the Self-sufficient, the Praised.

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: all that is in the heavens and on the earth belongs to Him. Surely, God is self-sufficient and praiseworthy.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and on earth: for verily Allah,- He is free of all wants, Worthy of all Praise.

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Lahu ma fee alssamawati wama fee alardi wainna Allaha lahuwa alghaniyyu alhameedu

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: Do you not see God has harnessed all that is in the earth, to your service? And the boats ply in the ocean by His command. He holds the sky in position lest it should fall upon the earth save by His dispensation. Verily God is compassionate and kind to men.

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: Did you not see that Allah has given in your control all that is in the earth – and the ship that moves upon the sea by His command? And He restricts the heavens that it may not fall on to the earth except by His command; indeed Allah is Most Compassionate, Most Merciful upon mankind.

65.author: Arberry
65.text: Hast thou not seen how that God has subjected to you all that is in the earth and the ships to run upon the sea at His commandment, and He holds back heaven lest it should fall upon the earth, save by His leave? Surely God is All-gentle to men, All-compassionate.

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: Beholdest thou not that Allah hath subjected to Himself for you whatsoever is on the earth and the ships running in the sea by His command? And He with holdeth the heaven that it fall not on the earth save by His leave. Verily Allah is, unto mankind, Clement, Merciful.

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: See you not that Allah has subjected to you (mankind) all that is on the earth, and the ships that sail through the sea by His Command? He withholds the heaven from falling on the earth except by His Leave. Verily, Allah is, for mankind, full of Kindness, Most Merciful.

65.author: Itani
65.text: Do you not see that God made everything on earth subservient to you? How the ships sail at sea by His command? That He holds up the sky lest it falls on earth—except by His permission? God is Gracious towards the people, Most Merciful.

65.author: Maududi
65.text: Have you not seen how Allah has subjected to you all that is in the earth, and the vessels that sail in the sea by His command, and it is He Who holds back the sky that it may not fall on earth except by His leave? Surely Allah is Most Gentle, Ever Compassionate to people.

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: See you not that Allah has subjected to you all that is on the earth, and the ships that sail through the sea by His command He withholds the heaven from falling on the earth except by His leave. Verily, Allah is for mankind, full of kindness, Most Merciful.

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: Hast thou not seen how Allah hath made all that is in the earth subservient unto you? And the ship runneth upon the sea by His command, and He holdeth back the heaven from falling on the earth unless by His leave. Lo! Allah is, for mankind, Full of Pity, Merciful.

65.author: Qarai
65.text: Have you not regarded that Allah has disposed for you [r benefit] whatever there is in the earth, and [that] the ships sail at sea by His command, and He sustains the sky lest it should fall on the earth, excepting [when it does so] by His leave? Indeed Allah is most kind and merciful to mankind.

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: Do you not see that Allah has subjected to you all that is on the earth, and the ships which run upon the sea by His command? He holds the sky back lest it should fall upon the earth except by His permission. Allah is Gentle to people, the Most Merciful.

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: Do you not see that Allah has subjected to you whatever is on the earth and the ships which run through the sea by His command? And He restrains the sky from falling upon the earth, unless by His permission. Indeed Allah, to the people, is Kind and Merciful.

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: Have you not seen that God, through His command, has made all that is in the earth and the ships that sail on the sea subservient to you? He prevents the sky from falling on the earth unless He decides otherwise. God is Compassionate and Merciful to mankind.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: Do you not see that Allah has made subservient to you whatsoever is in the earth and the ships running in the sea by His command? And He withholds the heaven from falling on the earth except with His permission; most surely Allah is Compassionate, Merciful to men.

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: Do you not see, how God has subjected everything on the earth to you, and the ships that sail on the sea by His command. He holds back the sky from falling down on the earth, except with His permission. God is most compassionate and most merciful to mankind --

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: Seest thou not that Allah has made subject to you (men) all that is on the earth, and the ships that sail through the sea by His Command? He withholds the sky (rain) from failing on the earth except by His leave: for Allah is Most Kind and Most Merciful to man.

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Alam tara anna Allaha sakhkhara lakum ma fee alardi waalfulka tajree fee albahri biamrihi wayumsiku alssamaa an taqaAAa AAala alardi illa biithnihi inna Allaha bialnnasi laraoofun raheemun

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: It is He who gives you life, then makes you die; then He will bring you back to life again. Man is surely most ungrateful.

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: And it is He Who gave you life, then will cause you to die, and will then revive you; indeed man is very ungrateful.

66.author: Arberry
66.text: It is He who gave you life, then He shall make you dead, then He shall give you life. Surely man is ungrateful.

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: And He it is who gave you life and will thereafter cause you to die, and will thereafter give you life; verily man is ingrate.

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: It is He, Who gave you life, and then will cause you to die, and will again give you life (on the Day of Resurrection). Verily! Man is indeed an ingrate.

66.author: Itani
66.text: And it is He who gives you life, then makes you die, then revives you. The human being is unappreciative.

66.author: Maududi
66.text: And it is He Who has endowed you with life and it is He who causes you to die, and it is He Who will then resurrect you. Man is indeed extremely prone to denying the Truth.

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: It is He, Who gave you life, and then will cause you to die, and will again give you life. Verily, man is indeed Kafurun.

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: And He it is Who gave you life, then He will cause you to die, and then will give you life (again). Lo! man is verily an ingrate.

66.author: Qarai
66.text: It is He who gave you life then He makes you die, then He brings you to life. Indeed man is very ungrateful.

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: It is He who revives you, then He will cause you to die and then He revives you. Indeed, the human is ungrateful.

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: And He is the one who gave you life; then He causes you to die and then will [again] give you life. Indeed, mankind is ungrateful.

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: It is He who has given you life, He will make you die and will make you live again. Surely the human being is ungrateful.

66.author: Shakir
66.text: And He it is Who has brought you to life, then He will cause you to die, then bring you to life (again); most surely man is ungrateful.

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: it is He who gave you life. Then He will cause you to die. Then He will give you life again. Surely, man is most ungrateful.

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: It is He Who gave you life, will cause you to die, and will again give you life: Truly man is a most ungrateful creature!

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Wahuwa allathee ahyakum thumma yumeetukum thumma yuhyeekum inna alinsana lakafoorun

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: We have determined for each community a way of worship which they follow. So they should not contend with you in this matter; and you should go on calling them to your Lord. You are surely on the right path.

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: For each nation We have made the rules of worship for them to follow – so never should they quarrel with you in this matter – and call them towards your Lord; indeed you are upon the Straight Path.

67.author: Arberry
67.text: We have appointed for every nation a holy rite that they shall perform. Let them not therefore wrangle with thee upon the matter, and do thou summon unto thy Lord; surely thou art upon a straight guidance.

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: Unto every community We have appointed a rite which they perform. Let them not therefore contend with thee in the affair; and call them thou unto thy Lord, verily thou art on true guidance.

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: For every nation We have ordained religious ceremonies [e.g. slaughtering of the beast of cattle during the three days of stay at Mina (Makkah) during the Hajj (pilgrimage)] which they must follow; so let them (pagans) not dispute with you on the matter (i.e. to eat of the cattle which you slaughter, and not to eat of cattle which Allah kills by its natural death), but invite them to your Lord. Verily! You (O Muhammad SAW) indeed are on the (true) straight guidance. (i.e. the true religion of Islamic Monotheism).

67.author: Itani
67.text: For every congregation We have appointed acts of devotion, which they observe. So do not let them dispute with you in this matter. And invite to your Lord; you are upon a straight guidance.

67.author: Maududi
67.text: For every people We have prescribed a way of worship which they follow. So, (O Muhammad), let them not dispute with you concerning this, and call them to Your Lord. You are certainly on the Straight Way.

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: For every nation We have made Mansak which they must follow; so let them not dispute with you on the matter, but invite them to your Lord. Verily, you indeed are on the straight guidance.

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: Unto each nation have We given sacred rites which they are to perform; so let them not dispute with thee of the matter, but summon thou unto thy Lord. Lo! thou indeed followest right guidance.

67.author: Qarai
67.text: For every nation We have appointed rites [of worship] which they observe; so let them not dispute with you concerning your religion, and invite to your Lord. Indeed, you are on a straight guidance.

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: For every nation We have appointed a Holy Rite which they shall perform. Do not let them dispute with you concerning the matter. Call to your Lord; surely, you are upon a Straight Path.

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: For every religion We have appointed rites which they perform. So, [O Muhammad], let the disbelievers not contend with you over the matter but invite them to your Lord. Indeed, you are upon straight guidance.

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: We enjoined every nation with certain worship acts which they perform. The unbelievers must not dispute with you about the manner of your worship. Invite them to follow the right path of the Lord.

67.author: Shakir
67.text: To every nation We appointed acts of devotion which they observe, therefore they should not dispute with you about the matter and call to your Lord; most surely you are on a right way.

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: We have appointed for every community ways of worship to observe. Let them not dispute with you on this matter. Call them to the path of your Lord -- for surely, you are rightly guided --

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: To every People have We appointed rites and ceremonies which they must follow: let them not then dispute with thee on the matter, but do thou invite (them) to thy Lord: for thou art assuredly on the Right Way.

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Likulli ommatin jaAAalna mansakan hum nasikoohu fala yunaziAAunnaka fee alamri waodAAu ila rabbika innaka laAAala hudan mustaqeemin

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: If they argue with you, tell them: "God knows well what you are doing.

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: And if they quarrel with you, say, “Allah well knows your evil deeds.”

68.author: Arberry
68.text: And if they should dispute with thee, do thou say, 'God knows very well what you are doing.

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: And if they dispute with thee, say thou: Allah knoweth best that which ye Work.

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: And if they argue with you (as regards the slaughtering of the sacrifices), say; "Allah knows best of what you do.

68.author: Itani
68.text: But if they dispute with you, say, “God is fully aware of what you do.”

68.author: Maududi
68.text: And if they dispute with you, say: "Allah knows well what you do.

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: And if they argue with you, say: "Allah knows best of what you do."

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: And if they wrangle with thee, say: Allah is Best Aware of what ye do.

68.author: Qarai
68.text: But if they dispute with you, say, ‘Allah knows best what you are doing.

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: If they argue with you, say: 'Allah knows best all that you do.

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: And if they dispute with you, then say, "Allah is most knowing of what you do.

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: If they still dispute with you about your worship, tell them, "God knows best what you do.

68.author: Shakir
68.text: And if they contend with you, say: Allah best knows what you do.

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: if they should dispute with you, then say, "God is well aware of what you do."

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: If they do wrangle with thee, say, "Allah knows best what it is ye are doing."

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Wain jadalooka faquli Allahu aAAlamu bima taAAmaloona

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: God will judge between you on the Day of Judgement in what you are at variance."

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning what you dispute.

69.author: Arberry
69.text: God shall judge between you on the Day of Resurrection touching that whereon you were at variance.'

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: Allah will judge between you on the Day of Judgment concerning that wherein ye have been differing.

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: "Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection about that wherein you used to differ."

69.author: Itani
69.text: God will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection regarding what you disagree about.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: Allah will judge among you on the Day of Resurrection concerning matters about which you disagreed."

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: "Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection about that wherein you used to differ."

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein ye used to differ.

69.author: Qarai
69.text: Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which you used to differ.

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will judge between you concerning that in which you vary.

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which you used to differ."

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: He will issue His decree about your differences on the Day of Judgment."

69.author: Shakir
69.text: Allah will judge between you on the day of resurrection respecting that in which you differ.

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: On the Day of Resurrection, God will judge between you regarding your differences.

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: "Allah will judge between you on the Day of Judgment concerning the matters in which ye differ."

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Allahu yahkumu baynakum yawma alqiyamati feema kuntum feehi takhtalifoona

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: Do you not know that God knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth? This is surely in accordance with the law. This is certainly how (the law of) God works inevitably.

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: Did you not realise that Allah knows all that is in the heavens and in the earth? Indeed all this is in a Book; indeed this is easy for Allah.

70.author: Arberry
70.text: Didst thou not know that God knows all that is in heaven and earth? Surely that is in a Book; surely that for God is an easy matter.

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: Knowest thou of that Allah knoweth whatsoever is in the heaven and the earth? Verily that is in a Book; verily that is for Allah easy.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: Know you not that Allah knows all that is in heaven and on earth? Verily, it is (all) in the Book (Al-Lauh Al-Mahfuz). Verily! That is easy for Allah.

70.author: Itani
70.text: Do you not know that God knows everything in the heavens and the earth? This is in a book. That is easy for God.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: Are you not aware that Allah knows all that is in the heaven and the earth? Surely it is all preserved in a Book. Indeed that is easy with Allah.

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: Know you not that Allah knows all that is in the heaven and on the earth Verily, it is (all) in the Book. Verily, that is easy for Allah.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: Hast thou not known that Allah knoweth all that is in the heaven and the earth? Lo! it is in a record. Lo! that is easy for Allah.

70.author: Qarai
70.text: Do you not know that Allah knows whatever there is in the heaven and the earth? All that is in a Book. That is indeed easy for Allah.’

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
